## Agent 3 — no actual lower \(<7\) independent \(Y12\) antecedent

I will use the current nomenclature:

\[
\rho_Y
\]

is the \(Y12\)-fiber graph slope,

\[
s_9^Y=\rho_Ys_7+\eta_{\mathrm{fixed}}.
\]

\[
\rho_\Gamma
=
\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}
\]

is the effective wall ratio after subtracting the \(\rho_Y\)-graph.

\[
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}
\]

is the O2/O3 fold ratio. I will not use \(r\) for these.

The task is to audit actual lower-order directions below order seven:

\[
\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith},
\]

and prove that they do not create an independent primitive \(Y12\)-fiber column. This matters because a formal \(C_7\to Y12\) connection does **not** by itself put \(\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y)\) into \(B_Y\); only actual-source motion does. 

---

# 1. The target statement

We need to classify

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}
\subset \mathbb C^2_{(s_7,s_9^Y)}.
\]

The desired theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}
\subseteq
0
\quad\text{or}\quad
\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y).
}
\]

More specifically, because these are lower than order seven, the expected result is the stronger statement:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}=0.
}
\]

If some lower direction survives, it must factor through the same \(C_7\)-graph line:

\[
(1,\rho_Y).
\]

The forbidden outcomes are:

\[
(0,1)\in \Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith},
\]

or two independent graph slopes

\[
(1,\rho_1),\qquad (1,\rho_2),\qquad \rho_1\ne\rho_2.
\]

Those would either kill the \(Y12\) vertical channel or make the local fiber actual-source model rank two.

---

# 2. Actual lower candidates versus formal carriers

Let

\[
U_{<7}^{\arith}
=
\{u_j\}
\]

be the finite list of actual lower-order directions below order seven.

Important: this set should include only actual-source/nuisance directions. It should **not** include formal D4 carrier labels unless those carriers are actual-source motions.

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
\text{formal lower carrier}
\neq
\text{actual lower tangent}.
}
\]

This is the correction from the latest round. A formal lower connection

\[
C_7\to Y12
\]

only says:

\[
\text{if }s_7\text{ is actual-source motion, then }s_9^Y=\rho_Ys_7.
\]

It does not prove \(s_7\in T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}\).

The lower \(<7\) audit must therefore use actual lower directions only.

---

# 3. Signature filter for lower candidates

For each candidate

\[
u_j\in U_{<7}^{\arith},
\]

assign the refined signature tuple

\[
\Sigma(u_j)
=
(\deg_{\src},o_N,o_W,\mathrm{mixed},\mathrm{same},w,\rho,\mathrm{carrier}).
\]

The primitive \(Y12\) signature is

\[
\Sigma(Y12)
=
(2,D^2N_0,D\mathcal W_{\same},12,\mathrm{same},1,\rho_Y,[\mathsf C_{Y12}]).
\]

For

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(u_j)
\]

to be nonzero, \(u_j\) must survive all of these filters:

1. source-Hessian degree \(2\);
2. nonlinear-source origin \(D^2N_0\);
3. whitening origin \(D\mathcal W_{\same}\);
4. mixed branch \(12\);
5. same-point support;
6. exactly one whitening slot;
7. correct reflection/source-grade parity;
8. compatible carrier class after transition to order nine.

Any failure gives

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(u_j)=0.
}
\]

This should kill most actual lower \(<7\) directions immediately.

---

# 4. Why lower \(<7\) should vanish

A lower \(<7\) actual-source direction has insufficient primitive order to carry

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]]
\]

as a live order-nine \(Y12\)-fiber coordinate unless it is transported by an allowed lower-connection mechanism.

But below order seven, there should be no live primitive Bottleneck-C antecedent with the full \(Y12\) signature. This is exactly the “first live primitive \(Y12\)” logic.

So the expected theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{NoActualLowerIndependentY12Antecedent.}
}
\]

No actual lower \(<7\) direction produces a primitive \(Y12\)-fiber coordinate independent of the \(C_7\) path.

Formally:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}
\subseteq
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_7^{\arith}.
}
\]

And if \(C_7\) is not actual-source tangent, then

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}=0.
}
\]

This distinction is essential.

---

# 5. Determinant/one-slope test for surviving lower candidates

Suppose some lower candidates survive the signature filter. For every pair

\[
u_i,u_j
\]

of surviving actual lower directions, compute

\[
\Delta_Y(u_i,u_j)
=
\ell_7(u_i)\ell_9^Y(u_j)
-
\ell_7(u_j)\ell_9^Y(u_i).
\]

The one-slope condition is:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_Y(u_i,u_j)=0
\quad
\text{for all surviving }u_i,u_j.
}
\]

If this holds and at least one \(u_j\) has

\[
\ell_7(u_j)\ne0,
\]

then all surviving lower directions lie in one graph line

\[
\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y)
\]

with

\[
\rho_Y=
\frac{\ell_9^Y(u_j)}{\ell_7(u_j)}.
\]

If all surviving \(u_j\) have

\[
\ell_7(u_j)=0
\]

but some have

\[
\ell_9^Y(u_j)\ne0,
\]

then a vertical lower antecedent exists:

\[
(0,1)\in B_Y.
\]

That is fatal.

If some pair has

\[
\Delta_Y(u_i,u_j)\ne0,
\]

then lower actual directions span two fiber slopes, and the current \(Y12\) route dies locally.

---

# 6. Bottleneck-C “first live” usage

The old first-live Bottleneck-C result should be used carefully.

It should not be used to assert:

\[
C_7\in T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}.
\]

Instead, it should be used to support:

\[
\boxed{
\text{there is no lower independent actual-source antecedent below the }C_7\text{ path.}
}
\]

In current notation:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}
\subseteq
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_7^{\arith}.
}
\]

So the first-live statement gives a **rank bound**, not existence of the graph line.

This is the main hidden connection from the recent synthesis.

---

# 7. Expected proof outline

The proof should proceed as:

## Step 1 — enumerate actual lower directions

List the actual lower \(<7\) directions, excluding D4-only formal carriers.

## Step 2 — apply origin-pair filter

Kill all directions not having

\[
(o_N,o_W)=(D^2N_0,D\mathcal W_{\same}).
\]

## Step 3 — apply mixed/same/whitening-slot filter

Kill all directions not mixed \(12\), same-point, one-whitening-slot.

## Step 4 — apply parity/reflection filter

Kill all directions with wrong source-grade reflection parity.

## Step 5 — use first-live Bottleneck-C ledger

Any survivor must factor through the unique \(C_7\)-path, if that path is actual-source admissible.

## Step 6 — determinant one-slope check

For remaining candidates, compute

\[
\Delta_Y(u_i,u_j)
\]

and prove it vanishes.

This yields the one-slope or zero result.

---

# 8. Failure modes

The theorem fails only if one of the following occurs.

## Failure A — hidden lower primitive \(Y12\) carrier

There exists an actual lower \(<7\) direction with the full primitive \(Y12\) signature.

This would contradict or refine the “first live” ledger.

## Failure B — lower nonlinear connection creates vertical \(Y12\)

Some lower direction has

\[
\ell_7(u)=0,\qquad \ell_9^Y(u)\ne0.
\]

Then \(Y12\) is actual-source coherent vertically; current route dies.

## Failure C — second slope

Two lower directions have

\[
\Delta_Y(u_i,u_j)\ne0.
\]

Then \(B_Y\) becomes rank two; current vertical \(Y12\) route dies.

## Failure D — quotient mismatch

The first-live Bottleneck-C statement lives in a different quotient and does not transfer to the current labelled singular fiber. Then the theorem needs a separate labelled-transition proof.

---

# 9. Current conclusion

The likely outcome is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}=0.
}
\]

or, at worst,

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}
\subseteq
\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y)
}
\]

conditional on \(s_7\) being actual-source motion.

Therefore lower \(<7\) directions should not by themselves prevent rank-two D4. The rank-two/rank-one fork remains governed primarily by whether the \(s_7\)-direction is actual-source tangent:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{s_7}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}=0
\quad\text{or not?}
}
\]

If zero, rank-two D4 remains viable.

If nonzero and lower continuation is one-slope, the rank-one moving wall route applies.

## Agent 3 continuation — lower \(<7\) audit refined by slot-count and order budget

I continue with the fixed nomenclature:

\[
\rho_Y
\]

is the \(Y12\)-fiber graph slope,

\[
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9},
\]

and

\[
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.
\]

The current question is whether actual lower \(<7\) directions can contribute an independent primitive \(Y12\)-fiber column. The key correction remains: a formal \(C_7\to Y12\) connection does not itself put \((1,\rho_Y)\) into \(B_Y\); only actual-source/nuisance directions do. 

---

# 1. Lower \(<7\) directions have a structural order-budget obstruction

The primitive \(Y12\) carrier has signature

\[
\Sigma(Y12)
=
(2,D^2N_0,D\mathcal W_{\same},12,\mathrm{same},w=1,\rho_Y,\operatorname{order}=9).
\]

A lower actual direction \(u\in\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}\) would need to acquire the full primitive \(Y12\) signature after transition/connection.

That requires all of:

\[
D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2],
\qquad
D\mathcal W_{\same},
\qquad
\text{mixed branch }12,
\qquad
\text{same-point support},
\qquad
\text{order }9.
\]

But a \(<7\) direction has too little order to supply the order-nine same-point \(D^2N_0\)-with-one-whitening slot unless a connection map contributes at least two additional orders **and** preserves the primitive origin.

So the lower \(<7\) audit should split into two cases:

\[
\boxed{
\text{pure transport / linear connection}
}
\]

versus

\[
\boxed{
\text{nonlinear recomputation that can generate }D^2N_0.
}
\]

Only the second is dangerous.

---

# 2. Linear lower transport cannot create primitive \(Y12\)

Suppose the lower-to-singular connection is built from linear transport, chart conversion, gauge conversion, and whitening transport applied to an already formed lower source expression.

Then origin is preserved.

A source-linear or lower non-\(D^2N_0\)-origin term remains non-\(D^2N_0\)-origin after transport. It cannot become

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]].
\]

Therefore:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}=0
}
\]

for all lower \(<7\) directions whose transition is formed-source linear.

This is the favorable default.

---

# 3. Nonlinear recomputation reduces to the same commutator test

A lower \(<7\) actual direction can become dangerous only if some transition/connection recomputes the nonlinear normal form:

\[
N_{0,\sing}(L\xi)
\]

instead of transporting the formed object

\[
S\,N_{0,\tr}(\xi).
\]

Then the difference contains the normal-form commutator

\[
\mathfrak C_Y(\xi_1,\xi_2)
=
\Pi_Y\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
-
S D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\right),
\]

plus possible \(DN_0[D^2L]\) and whitening-transport defects.

Thus any dangerous lower \(<7\) contribution is not a separate lower-connection phenomenon. It is part of the labelled-realization/normal-form commutator audit.

So:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}\neq0
\Rightarrow
\mathfrak C_Y\text{ or an equivalent recomputation defect is nonzero.}
}
\]

That means the lower \(<7\) and labelled-transition audits are coupled.

---

# 4. Slot-count obstruction for actual second curvature below order seven

A lower \(<7\) actual direction could also try to reach \(Y12\) through actual EF second curvature. But the slot-rank test blocks this unless actual first-variation spaces contain all required primitive slots:

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}[a_Y]\,D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2].
\]

Let the actual first-variation slot spaces be

\[
V_W,\quad V_1,\quad V_2.
\]

The dangerous inclusion is

\[
a_Y\in V_W,\qquad \xi_1\in V_1,\qquad \xi_2\in V_2.
\]

For fixed-\(\alpha\) downstream, ordinary actual parameters \(m,\lambda\), phase, normalization, background/gamma are source-linear or diagonal. They should not span the mixed \(12\) pair plus same-point whitening slot.

Thus the expected vanishing is

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0,
}
\]

and therefore lower \(<7\) directions cannot enter through second curvature.

---

# 5. Refined lower \(<7\) theorem

The clean theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{NoActualLowerIndependentY12Antecedent}^{<7}.
}
\]

Assume:

1. Lower \(<7\) actual-source directions are transported as formed labelled sources, not recomputed through \(N_0\).

2. The normal-form commutator satisfies

\[
\mathfrak C_Y=0
\]

or contributes only finite coherent / residual-controlled amplitudes, not local fiber columns.

3. Actual EF second curvature fails the primitive slot-rank test:

\[
DW_{\same}[V_W]D^2N_0[V_1,V_2]
\cap
\Sigma(Y12)=0.
\]

4. The singular nuisance quotient is origin-faithful near \(\Sigma(Y12)\).

Then

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}=0.
}
\]

If condition 1 fails, any nonzero lower \(<7\) image must be routed through the commutator classification:

\[
0,\quad \mathbb C(1,\rho_Y),\quad (0,1),\quad \mathbb C^2,\quad \mathcal C_Y^{\arith},\quad R_X^Y.
\]

---

# 6. What if a lower \(<7\) survivor exists?

Suppose an actual lower \(<7\) direction \(u\) survives:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(u)=(a,b)\neq0.
\]

There are four outcomes.

## 6.1 Pure \(s_7\)

\[
(a,b)=(a,0),\qquad a\neq0.
\]

This creates an actual \(s_7\) tangent with slope \(0\). If there is also a formal/actual \(C_7\to Y12\) graph of slope \(\rho_Y\), the two must be reconciled.

If the graph continuation is mandatory, then pure \(s_7\) is inconsistent with one-slope coherence unless \(\rho_Y=0\).

## 6.2 One-slope graph

\[
(a,b)=a(1,\rho_Y).
\]

Safe for rank-one; rank-two unavailable.

## 6.3 Vertical

\[
(a,b)=(0,b),\qquad b\neq0.
\]

Fatal for the \(Y12\) vertical channel.

## 6.4 Second slope

Two lower survivors \(u,v\) satisfy

\[
\Delta_Y(u,v)
=
\ell_7(u)\ell_9^Y(v)-\ell_7(v)\ell_9^Y(u)
\neq0.
\]

Then \(B_Y\) has rank two and the current \(Y12\) vertical route dies locally.

Thus if any lower survivor exists, compute the determinant test:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_Y(u,v)=0
\quad\forall u,v.
}
\]

---

# 7. Consequence for rank-two rescue

The lower \(<7\) result directly affects rank two.

If

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}=0
\]

and \(C_7\) is not actual-source tangent, then lower actual directions do not enter \(B_Y\). This supports

\[
B_Y=0
\]

and makes rank-two D4 plausible.

If

\[
C_7
\]

is actual-source tangent and lower continuation is one-slope, then

\[
B_Y=\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y)
\]

and rank one is forced.

So the conclusion is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Lower }<7\text{ directions should not decide rank two versus rank one.}
}
\]

The decisive rank-two/rank-one fork remains:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{s_7}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}=0\quad\text{or not?}
}
\]

---

# 8. Updated audit order

For the lower side, the correct finite audit order is:

1. Enumerate actual lower \(<7\) directions only.

2. Apply origin-pair filter.

3. Apply slot-rank filter.

4. Check whether any nonzero contribution is actually a labelled-transition commutator defect.

5. If nonzero fiber columns remain, compute

\[
\Delta_Y(u,v).
\]

6. Route result:

\[
0,\quad \mathbb C(1,\rho_Y),\quad (0,1),\quad \mathbb C^2.
\]

This gives a deterministic contribution to \(B_Y\).

---

# 9. Current endpoint

The best current theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}=0
}
\]

under labelled formed-source transition and fixed-\(\alpha\) actual-source parameters.

If this theorem holds, then lower \(<7\) directions do not obstruct rank-two D4. The rank-two fork is controlled by whether \(s_7\) itself is actual-source motion:

\[
\boxed{
C_7\text{ actual-source tangent or D4-only carrier?}
}
\]

That remains the next decisive finite audit.

## Agent 3 continuation — testing whether \(s_7\) is actual-source motion

I continue with the fixed nomenclature:

\[
\rho_Y
\]

is the \(Y12\)-fiber graph slope,

\[
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9},
\]

and

\[
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.
\]

The raw graph-subtracted wall is

\[
\ell_{\raw}(\Gamma)=\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G).
\]

The prior step showed that lower \(<7\) actual directions probably vanish in the primitive \(Y12\)-fiber, unless they arise through labelled-transition recomputation defects. The decisive remaining fork is now:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{s_7}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}=0
\quad\text{or not?}
}
\]

Equivalently: is the \(C_7\) / \(s_7\) direction an actual-source tangent, or only a D4/formal carrier? This is now the main rank-two versus rank-one decision point. 

---

# 1. Define the \(s_7\)-projection test

Let

\[
\ell_7
\]

be the live dual covector satisfying

\[
\ell_7(e_7)=1,\qquad \ell_7(e_Y)=0.
\]

Define

\[
\Pi_{s_7}(v):=\ell_7(v).
\]

The actual-source \(s_7\)-test is:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(u)=0
\quad
\text{for every }
u\in T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}.
}
\]

If true, then actual-source rows do not move in the \(s_7\)-direction. The formal \(C_7\to Y12\) graph remains merely formal and does not enter \(B_Y\). Then, assuming no other local fiber columns survive,

\[
B_Y=0,
\]

and rank-two D4 remains available.

If false, choose an actual tangent \(u_7^{\arith}\) with

\[
\ell_7(u_7^{\arith})\ne0.
\]

Then one must test whether

\[
\ell_9^Y(u_7^{\arith})
=
\rho_Y\ell_7(u_7^{\arith})
\]

for a unique low-order \(\rho_Y\). If yes, \(B_Y=\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y)\), and the route is rank one.

---

# 2. Formal \(C_7\) carrier versus actual tangent

The formal D4 package contains

\[
e_7
=
\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}[\mathsf C_7].
\]

But the existence of \(e_7\) means only:

\[
\boxed{
C_7\text{ is a formal/live carrier coordinate.}
}
\]

It does **not** imply

\[
e_7\in T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}.
\]

The latter requires an admissible actual-source deformation whose singular realization has nonzero \(e_7\)-coordinate.

So the correct test is:

\[
\boxed{
e_7\in \operatorname{span}
\left(
\mathcal N_{\sing},
\mathcal T_{\par},
\mathcal T_7^{\arith},
\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith},
\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9},
\mathcal T_{E_2}^{\local}
\right)
?
}
\]

But with origin filtering, most of these spaces should not hit \(e_7\).

---

# 3. Audit sources of actual \(s_7\)-motion

## 3.1 First-order actual parameters

For actual EF parameters

\[
m,\lambda,\text{phase},\text{normalization},\text{background/gamma},
\]

the variations are source-linear / EF-linear. The expected result is

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal T_{\par})=0.
}
\]

Reason: \(s_7\) is a carrier coordinate in the same finite D4/Y12 package, not a generic phase/cutoff derivative.

If an \(\alpha\)-parameter is only proof-thickening for O2/O3, it should not be included in \(T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}\). If \(\alpha\) is admitted as an actual-source deformation, it requires a separate slot audit.

## 3.2 Lower actual coherence

The real risk is

\[
\mathcal T_7^{\arith}.
\]

We need to know whether the \(C_7\) direction is actual-source coherent. There are two possibilities:

### D4-only \(C_7\)

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal T_7^{\arith})=0.
}
\]

Then \(C_7\) appears only when D4 perturbs the source; it is not a direction of the actual-source manifold.

This supports rank two.

### Actual-source \(C_7\)

There is

\[
u_7^{\arith}\in\mathcal T_7^{\arith}
\]

with

\[
\ell_7(u_7^{\arith})\ne0.
\]

Then rank two is lost unless the actual-source image is still zero in the quotient for some other reason. One must test the slope:

\[
\rho_Y
=
\frac{\ell_9^Y(u_7^{\arith})}{\ell_7(u_7^{\arith})}.
\]

If this is unique, rank one survives.

## 3.3 Lower \(<7\)

The previous audit supports

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith})=0
}
\]

or at worst factoring through the same \(C_7\) line if \(C_7\) is actual-source allowed.

Thus lower \(<7\) should not independently create \(s_7\)-motion.

## 3.4 Actual second curvature

For fixed-\(\alpha\), the slot-rank argument should give

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0.
}
\]

If actual second curvature creates \(s_7\), then again one must test whether its \(s_9^Y\)-coordinate lies on the same graph. A different slope would kill the route.

## 3.5 \(E_2^Y\)

The coefficient-map audit gives:

- fixed \(E_2^Y\): no tangent;
- graph-only \(E_2^Y\): possible \(s_7\)-graph contribution;
- finite coherent \(E_2^Y\): amplitude model, not local \(s_7\)-tangent;
- locally free \(E_2^Y\): fatal.

So \(E_2^Y\) contributes to \(\Pi_{s_7}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}\) only if its local derivative map has nonzero \(s_7\)-coordinate.

---

# 4. A concrete rank-two sufficient theorem

The rank-two sufficient theorem is now:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{NoActualS7Motion}_{Y12}.
}
\]

Assume:

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0,
\]

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal T_{\par})=0,
\]

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal T_7^{\arith})=0,
\]

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith})=0,
\]

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0,
\]

and

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal T_{E_2}^{\local})=0.
\]

Then

\[
\Pi_{s_7}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}=0.
\]

If in addition the vertical \(s_9^Y\) actual-source tangent also vanishes,

\[
\ell_9^Y(T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith})=0,
\]

then

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

Therefore D4 has rank two:

\[
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Since

\[
c_7\alpha_9\ne0,
\]

this is polynomially equivalent to

\[
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]

---

# 5. A concrete rank-one sufficient theorem

If

\[
\Pi_{s_7}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}\ne0,
\]

rank two is not available. The rank-one theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{OneSlopeActualS7}_{Y12}.
}
\]

Assume every actual-source tangent \(u\) with

\[
\ell_7(u)\ne0
\]

satisfies

\[
\ell_9^Y(u)=\rho_Y\ell_7(u)
\]

for the same \(\rho_Y\), and every actual-source tangent \(v\) with

\[
\ell_7(v)=0
\]

also has

\[
\ell_9^Y(v)=0.
\]

Then

\[
B_Y=\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y).
\]

The forbidden covector is

\[
\ell_Y=\ell_9^Y-\rho_Y\ell_7,
\]

and D4 gives

\[
\ell_Y(D4)
=
H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G).
\]

This is the rank-one moving wall route.

---

# 6. Finite determinant test for actual \(s_7\)-motion

Let

\[
u_1,\ldots,u_M
\]

be a finite basis of actual-source/nuisance candidates in the live fiber.

Compute

\[
a_j=\ell_7(u_j),\qquad b_j=\ell_9^Y(u_j).
\]

Then:

## Rank-two rescue

\[
a_j=b_j=0
\quad\forall j.
\]

## Rank-one graph

There exists \(\rho_Y\) such that

\[
b_j=\rho_Ya_j
\quad\forall j,
\]

and not all \(a_j\) vanish.

Equivalently,

\[
a_i b_j-a_j b_i=0
\quad\forall i,j.
\]

## Fatal vertical

There exists \(j\) with

\[
a_j=0,\qquad b_j\ne0.
\]

## Fatal rank two

There exist \(i,j\) with

\[
a_i b_j-a_j b_i\ne0.
\]

So \(B_Y\)-classification is a finite \(2\times M\) rank test.

---

# 7. Current best expectation

Given the previous origin-filter arguments:

\[
\mathcal T_{\par}
\]

should vanish;

\[
\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}
\]

should vanish;

\[
\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}
\]

should vanish for fixed-\(\alpha\);

\[
E_2^Y
\]

should be fixed/residual/finite coherent but not local tangent;

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
\]

should not touch the live pair if the quotient is faithful.

Thus the plausible favorable outcome is:

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

This is stronger than the previously assumed rank-one default.

The only serious obstacle is whether

\[
\mathcal T_7^{\arith}
\]

contains the actual \(C_7\) direction.

So the key remaining audit is:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal T_7^{\arith})=0
\quad\text{or not?}
}
\]

---

# 8. What would make \(C_7\) actual-source tangent?

\(C_7\) is actual-source tangent only if an admissible source deformation changes the order-seven carrier coordinate while remaining within the actual-source manifold.

Possible mechanisms:

1. actual lower connection / source-family parameter controls \(C_7\);
2. \(E_2\) or chart normalization introduces a local \(C_7\)-amplitude;
3. singular nuisance quotient treats \(C_7\) as allowed;
4. \(\alpha\) or another proof parameter is included as actual and moves \(C_7\).

Each must be checked. If none hold, \(C_7\) is D4-only.

---

# 9. Current endpoint

The downstream fork is now reduced to a very concrete finite question:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal T_7^{\arith})=0?
}
\]

If yes, and all other actual-source contributions vanish in the live fiber, then

\[
B_Y=0
\]

and rank-two D4 should be pursued immediately.

If no, then compute

\[
\rho_Y=\frac{\ell_9^Y(u_7^{\arith})}{\ell_7(u_7^{\arith})}
\]

and test one-slope consistency. If one-slope holds, rank one survives; if not, the current \(Y12\) route dies locally.

## Agent 3 continuation — auditing whether \(C_7\) is actual-source tangent

I continue with the fixed nomenclature:

\[
\rho_Y
\]

is the \(Y12\)-fiber graph slope,

\[
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9},
\]

and

\[
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.
\]

The graph-subtracted wall is

\[
\ell_{\raw}(\Gamma)
=
\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G).
\]

The decisive local fork is now:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal T_7^{\arith})=0
\quad\text{or not?}
}
\]

Equivalently: is the \(C_7\) coordinate actual-source motion, or only a D4/formal carrier? This distinction controls rank-two versus rank-one D4. 

---

# 1. What \(\mathcal T_7^{\arith}\) should mean

Define

\[
\mathcal T_7^{\arith}
\]

as the part of the actual-source tangent/allowed model whose realized singular fiber has order-seven live coordinate \(s_7\). It should include only genuine actual-source motions:

\[
\partial_\theta q''_{\can},
\qquad
\theta\in\Theta_{\arith},
\]

plus legitimate nuisance or chart directions explicitly declared coherent.

It should **not** include:

\[
C_7
\]

merely because \(C_7\) appears as a formal D4 carrier.

That is the critical separation:

\[
\boxed{
C_7\text{ as D4 carrier}
\neq
C_7\text{ as actual-source tangent}.
}
\]

So the actual test is:

\[
\boxed{
\exists\theta\in\Theta_{\arith}
\quad
\ell_7(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})\neq0?
}
\]

If no, then \(s_7\) is not actual-source motion.

---

# 2. Candidate sources of \(s_7\)-motion

We should audit each admissible source of actual tangent.

## 2.1 \(m\)-translation

A height shift gives

\[
\partial_m e^{-im\log n}
=
-i\log n\,e^{-im\log n}.
\]

This is EF-linear. It changes phase weights but does not create the finite D4 carrier \(C_7\) unless \(C_7\) is itself part of the source-linear EF span.

Expected:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\partial_m q''_{\can})=0.
}
\]

If nonzero, that would mean the order-seven D4 carrier is already present in ordinary phase differentiation, which would strongly suggest it is not a forbidden D4 carrier.

## 2.2 \(\lambda\)-scaling

A scale change affects local coordinates:

\[
x\mapsto \lambda x,
\]

and creates Taylor-rescaling derivatives. Again, this is chart/source-linear unless scaling recomputes the nonlinear finite D4 package.

Expected:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\partial_\lambda q''_{\can})=0.
}
\]

A nonzero value should be treated as chart-coherence, not automatically as actual \(C_7\)-motion, unless it survives the labelled carrier projection.

## 2.3 Phase/cutoff/normalization

These are EF-linear proof or source-normalization variations. They should not touch \(C_7\) in the primitive D4 live-pair fiber.

Expected:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\partial_{\mathrm{phase}}q''_{\can})
=
\ell_7(\partial_{\mathrm{cutoff}}q''_{\can})
=
\ell_7(\partial_{\mathrm{norm}}q''_{\can})
=
0.
}
\]

If cutoff is a proof choice rather than actual source parameter, it should not be included in \(\Theta_{\arith}\) at all.

## 2.4 \(\alpha\)-tilt

If \(\alpha\) is used only as O2/O3 proof-thickening, it is not an actual-source tangent and should be excluded from \(\mathcal T_7^{\arith}\).

If \(\alpha\) is admitted as an actual-source parameter, then audit:

\[
\ell_7(\partial_\alpha q''_{\can}),
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\partial_\alpha q''_{\can}).
\]

Safe cases:

\[
(0,0)
\quad\text{or}\quad
\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y).
\]

Fatal cases:

\[
(0,1)
\quad\text{or a second slope.}
\]

Current preference:

\[
\boxed{
\alpha\text{ should remain proof-thickening, not actual-source tangent, unless forced.}
}
\]

## 2.5 \(E_2^Y\) local model

A local \(E_2^Y\) contribution can create \(s_7\)-motion only through its coefficient-map derivative:

\[
\mathcal F_E(\delta\theta)
=
(\delta s_7,\delta s_9^Y).
\]

Expected favorable result:

\[
\boxed{
\delta s_7=0
}
\]

for all \(E_2^Y\) residual/coherent amplitude variations, except graph-compatible corrections already included in \(\rho_Y\).

If \(E_2^Y\) contributes

\[
(1,\rho_Y),
\]

then \(B_Y\) becomes one-slope rank one.

If it contributes

\[
(0,1)
\]

or another slope, the \(Y12\) route dies locally.

---

# 3. The finite matrix test

Let

\[
u_1,\ldots,u_M
\]

be a basis of all actual-source/nuisance candidate directions after origin filtering.

Define

\[
a_j:=\ell_7(u_j),
\qquad
b_j:=\ell_9^Y(u_j).
\]

Then:

## Rank-two D4 possible

\[
a_j=b_j=0
\quad\forall j.
\]

Then

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

## Rank-one graph

There exists \(\rho_Y\) such that

\[
b_j=\rho_Ya_j
\quad\forall j,
\]

and at least one \(a_j\neq0\). Equivalently,

\[
a_i b_j-a_j b_i=0
\quad\forall i,j.
\]

Then

\[
B_Y=\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y).
\]

## Fatal vertical

There exists \(j\) with

\[
a_j=0,\qquad b_j\neq0.
\]

Then

\[
(0,1)\in B_Y.
\]

## Fatal rank two

There exist \(i,j\) with

\[
a_i b_j-a_j b_i\neq0.
\]

Then

\[
\operatorname{rank}B_Y=2.
\]

This is now a completely finite audit.

---

# 4. What would prove \(C_7\) is D4-only?

It is enough to show:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})=0
\quad
\forall\theta\in\Theta_{\arith}.
}
\]

A structural way to prove this is by origin/type:

1. \(\partial_\theta q''_{\can}\) is EF-linear, background, or chart-linear.
2. \(C_7\) is a finite D4 carrier produced by the D4 source insertion.
3. The live dual \(\ell_7\) is chosen to kill source-linear/background/chart directions.

Then

\[
\ell_7(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})=0.
\]

This is the clean theorem:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{NoActualS7Motion.}
}
\]

If it holds, and if no vertical \(s_9^Y\)-motion exists, then

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

---

# 5. What would prove \(C_7\) is actual-source tangent?

One must exhibit an admissible actual parameter or coherent model direction

\[
u_7^{\arith}
\]

such that

\[
\ell_7(u_7^{\arith})\neq0.
\]

Then compute

\[
\rho_Y^{\arith}
=
\frac{\ell_9^Y(u_7^{\arith})}{\ell_7(u_7^{\arith})}.
\]

To be safe, this slope must be:

\[
\rho_Y^{\arith}\in\mathcal A_{\low},
\]

and must not depend on

\[
\Gamma_{G,2},\quad q^{(4)},\quad s_9^Y,
\]

or fitted order-nine data.

Then one-slope consistency requires every other actual-source fiber direction \(u\) to satisfy

\[
\ell_9^Y(u)=\rho_Y^{\arith}\ell_7(u).
\]

If this holds, rank-one D4 survives.

---

# 6. Best current expectation

Given the source-origin audits already made:

\[
\mathcal T_{\par}
\]

should not hit \(s_7\);

\[
\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}
\]

should not hit \(s_7\);

\[
\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}
\]

should vanish in this primitive fiber for fixed-\(\alpha\);

\[
E_2^Y
\]

should be fixed/residual/finite coherent, not local fiber tangent;

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
\]

should not touch the live pair if the quotient is faithful.

Therefore the current expected favorable outcome is:

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

This means rank-two D4 may be more plausible than the earlier rank-one default.

The only serious possible spoiler is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal T_7^{\arith}\text{ contains an actual }C_7\text{ tangent.}
}
\]

---

# 7. Consequence if \(B_Y=0\)

If

\[
B_Y=0,
\]

D4 gives the vector wall

\[
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Since

\[
c_7\alpha_9\neq0,
\]

this is equivalent, up to polynomial conditioning, to

\[
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]

Then the downstream wall theorem becomes a joint anisotropy/sublevel theorem:

\[
\boxed{
\int
\min\left(
Q^A
\frac{|H|^2(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2)}{\mathcal N_{D4}},
1
\right)
d\nu
\ge
Q^{-B}\nu.
}
\]

No moving-line wall, no \(\rho_\Gamma\), and no forced ODE trap are needed at rank-two level.

This is a major simplification.

---

# 8. Consequence if \(B_Y=\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y)\)

Then the one-dimensional forbidden covector is

\[
\ell_Y=\ell_9^Y-\rho_Y\ell_7.
\]

The D4 wall is

\[
H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G).
\]

Then all the previous rank-one apparatus is required:

1. \(\rho_Y,\rho_\Gamma\in\mathcal A_{\low}\);
2. amplitude Schur if \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\neq0\);
3. wall finite type;
4. trap-Schur if wall finite type fails.

Rank-one remains viable but harder.

---

# 9. Current endpoint

The attack now reduces to one explicit question:

\[
\boxed{
\exists u\in T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
\quad
\ell_7(u)\neq0?
}
\]

If no, pursue rank-two D4.

If yes, compute the unique slope \(\rho_Y\) and test one-slope consistency.

This is the next decisive finite audit.

# 1. Actual lower directions versus formal D4 carriers

\[
\mathcal T_7^{\arith},\qquad \mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}

to include only genuine actual-source or nuisance tangent directions. They should exclude:

\[
\text{formal D4 carrier variables},
\]

\[

\]

\[
\text{formal lower connection directions without actual-source antecedent}.
\]

Thus the object under audit is not the formal statement

\[
C_7\leadsto C_{Y12},
\]

but the actual-source statement

\[
\exists u\in T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
\quad
\ell_7(u)\ne0
\quad\text{or}\quad
\ell_9^Y(u)\ne0?
\]

The distinction is essential.

---

# 2. Signature filter for lower candidates

Let

\[
u_j\in \mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}
\]

be an actual lower candidate. Associate the refined signature

\[
\Sigma(u_j)
=
(\deg_{\src},o_N,o_W,\mathrm{mixed},\mathrm{same},w,\rho,\mathrm{carrier}).
\]

To enter the primitive \(Y12\)-fiber, \(u_j\) must match

\[
\Sigma(Y12)
=
(2,D^2N_0,D\mathcal W_{\same},12,\mathrm{same},1,\rho_Y,[\mathsf C_{Y12}]).
\]

Therefore any lower candidate missing one of the following is killed:

\[
\deg_{\src}=2,
\]

\[
o_N=D^2N_0,
\]

\[
o_W=D\mathcal W_{\same},
\]

\[
\mathrm{mixed}=12,
\]

\[
\mathrm{same\ point},
\]

\[
w=1,
\]

and the correct source-grade/reflection parity.

For all such candidates,

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(u_j)=0.
}
\]

This should kill ordinary lower actual directions.

---

# 3. Order-budget obstruction below seven

A primitive \(Y12\) coordinate is an order-nine object. It contains:

\[
D\mathcal W_{\same}
\left[D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]\right],
\]

with mixed branch \(12\), same-point support, and one whitening slot.

An actual lower \(<7\) direction cannot become such an order-nine primitive carrier by ordinary formed-source transport. To do so, a connection would have to:

1. add at least two effective orders;
2. create or preserve \(D^2N_0\)-origin;
3. preserve the same-point whitening role;
4. preserve the mixed \(12\) parity.

A linear/formed-source transport cannot create the missing primitive nonlinear origin. Therefore, under labelled transition,

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}=0.
}
\]

The only possible exception is if the transition recomputes \(N_0\). Then the contribution belongs to the normal-form commutator audit, not to ordinary lower connection.

---

# 4. Lower \(<7\) and the normal-form commutator

If a lower \(<7\) actual direction survives the signature filter, then it must arise from a recomputation defect:

\[
N_{0,\sing}(L\xi)
\quad\text{versus}\quad
S N_{0,\tr}(\xi).
\]

The relevant commutator is

\[
\mathfrak C_Y(\xi_1,\xi_2)
=
\Pi_Y
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
-
S D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\right),
\]

plus any \(DN_0[D^2L]\) and whitening-transport defects.

Thus

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}\ne0
\Rightarrow
\mathfrak C_Y\ne0
}
\]

in the relevant primitive sector, unless there is an explicit actual lower primitive \(Y12\) direction.

So lower \(<7\) survival is not a separate mechanism; it is a labelled-realization failure or a hidden actual primitive antecedent.

---

# 5. The \(C_7\) order-seven case

Now audit

\[
\mathcal T_7^{\arith}.
\]

There are two distinct facts:

## Formal fact

There may be a formal lower connection

\[
e_7\mapsto e_7+\rho_Ye_Y.
\]

This defines the conditional slope

\[
\rho_Y.
\]

## Actual-source fact

This formal slope enters \(B_Y\) only if there is an actual-source tangent

\[
u_7^{\arith}\in\mathcal T_7^{\arith}
\]

with

\[
\ell_7(u_7^{\arith})\ne0.
\]

So the rank-two fork is controlled by

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal T_7^{\arith})=0\quad\text{or not}.
}
\]

If

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal T_7^{\arith})=0,
\]

then the \(C_7\)-path is D4-only, and the formal \(C_7\to Y12\) connection does not enter \(B_Y\).

If nonzero, one must check one-slope continuation:

\[
\ell_9^Y(u_7^{\arith})=\rho_Y\ell_7(u_7^{\arith}).
\]

---

# 6. Determinant test for surviving actual lower directions

For any surviving actual lower directions

\[
u_i,u_j,
\]

compute

\[
\Delta_Y(u_i,u_j)
=
\ell_7(u_i)\ell_9^Y(u_j)
-
\ell_7(u_j)\ell_9^Y(u_i).
\]

Then:

- all columns zero:

\[
\ell_7(u_j)=\ell_9^Y(u_j)=0
\quad\forall j
\]

gives no \(B_Y\)-contribution;

- one-slope:

\[
\Delta_Y(u_i,u_j)=0
\quad\forall i,j
\]

and some \(\ell_7(u_j)\ne0\) gives

\[
B_Y\supseteq \mathbb C(1,\rho_Y);
\]

- vertical:

\[
\ell_7(u)=0,\quad \ell_9^Y(u)\ne0
\]

kills the \(Y12\) vertical channel;

- two slopes:

\[
\Delta_Y(u_i,u_j)\ne0
\]

gives rank-two actual-source fiber and kills the current \(Y12\) route.

---

# 7. Use of Bottleneck-C “first live” information

The first-live Bottleneck-C statement should be used only to show:

\[
\boxed{
\text{no lower actual direction below }C_7
\text{ gives an independent primitive }Y12\text{ fiber column.}
}
\]

It should not be used to assert:

\[
C_7\in T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}.
\]

So the correct inference is:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}
\subseteq
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_7^{\arith},
\]

not

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_7^{\arith}\ne0.
\]

This supports rank-two if \(\mathcal T_7^{\arith}\) itself vanishes.

---

# 8. Theorem statement

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{NoActualLowerFiberMotion}_{<7}.
}
\]

Assume:

1. lower \(<7\) actual directions are formed-source transported, not recomputed through \(N_0\);

2. the normal-form commutator \(\mathfrak C_Y\) is zero, finite coherent, or residual-controlled, not local fiber freedom;

3. the singular quotient preserves the origin pair near \(\Sigma(Y12)\);

4. the first-live primitive \(Y12\) ledger excludes lower independent antecedents.

Then

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}=0.
}
\]

For order-seven actual directions, the theorem is conditional:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_7^{\arith}=0
\quad\text{unless }C_7\text{ is actual-source motion.}
}
\]

If \(C_7\) is actual-source motion, then any \(Y12\) continuation must be checked by the one-slope determinant condition.

---

# 9. Consequences

## If \(C_7\) is not actual-source motion

Then lower actual directions do not contribute to \(B_Y\):

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(\mathcal T_7^{\arith}+\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith})=0.
\]

This supports

\[
B_Y=0
\]

and rank-two D4.

## If \(C_7\) is actual-source motion

Then lower contribution is at most

\[
\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y)
\]

provided the one-slope theorem holds. Rank one is the route.

## If lower vertical/second slope occurs

The current \(Y12\) route dies locally or requires a different carrier/quotient.

---

# 10. Current endpoint

The lower \(<7\) audit is favorable for rank two:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}=0
}
\]

under origin-faithful labelled transition.

The order-seven piece reduces to the single decisive question:

\[
\boxed{
C_7\text{ actual-source tangent or D4-only?}
}
\]

Thus lower directions below seven should not block rank-two D4.

}

If it vanishes, rank-two D4 remains viable. If it does not vanish and the continuation is one-slope, the route collapses to rank one.

---

# 1. What \(C_7\) actualness means

Let

\[
e_7=\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}[\mathsf C_7]
\]

be the live order-seven carrier. The question is not whether \(e_7\) exists. It does. The question is whether there exists an admissible actual-source deformation

\[
u_\theta=\partial_\theta q''_{\can}
\]

such that

\[
\ell_7(u_\theta)\neq0.
\]

So \(C_7\) is actual-source motion iff

\[
\boxed{
e_7
\in
\operatorname{span}
\left(
\Pi_{\live}\mathcal T_{\par},
\Pi_{\live}\mathcal T_7^{\arith},
\Pi_{\live}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9},
\Pi_{\live}\mathcal T_{E_2}^{\local},
\Pi_{\live}\mathcal T_{\lab}^{\local},
\Pi_{\live}\mathcal N_{\sing}
\right)
}
\]

in the live \((e_7,e_Y)\)-quotient.

If all actual/nuisance/local images are killed by \(\ell_7\), then \(C_7\) is D4-only.

---

# 2. Origin filter for \(\ell_7\)

The live \(C_7\) carrier should also have a refined origin label, not merely “order seven.”

Call its signature

\[
\Sigma(C_7)
=
(\deg_{\src},o_N,o_W,\mathrm{branch},\mathrm{same},w,\rho,\mathrm{order}=7,[\mathsf C_7]).
\]

The exact labels need the paper’s internal definition, but the audit principle is clear:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(v)\neq0
\quad\Rightarrow\quad
v\text{ must have the live }C_7\text{ carrier signature.}
}
\]

Therefore ordinary actual-source variations can contain order-seven Taylor coefficients and still satisfy

\[
\ell_7(v)=0
\]

if they do not carry the live \(C_7\)-carrier class.

This prevents the common false inference:

\[
\text{order-seven term exists}
\Rightarrow
s_7\text{ actual}.
\]

The correct implication is:

\[
\text{live }C_7\text{ carrier signature exists in an actual tangent}
\Rightarrow
s_7\text{ actual}.
\]

---

# 3. Candidate actual sources of \(s_7\)

## 3.1 EF parameter tangents

For

\[
m,\lambda,\text{phase},\text{normalization},\text{chart},\text{background/gamma},
\]

the derivative of the canonical source is source-linear / EF-linear / chart-linear. These variations should be killed by the live \(C_7\) covector:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal T_{\par}^{\actual})=0.
}
\]

This is the first major favorable input for rank two.

The proof is by origin:

\[
\operatorname{Origin}(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})
\in
\{\mathrm{EF\text{-}linear},\mathrm{chart},\mathrm{background}\},
\]

whereas \(e_7\) is a finite D4 carrier, not generic EF-linear order-seven content.

## 3.2 Actual second curvature

For fixed-\(\alpha\), second actual curvature should also be killed:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0.
}

The slot-rank reason is the same as for \(Y12\): ordinary actual parameter variations do not supply the required live carrier slots. If \(\alpha\) is excluded from actual-source motion and used only as proof-thickening, this vanishing becomes more plausible.

## 3.3 \(E_2\)

Fixed or residual \(E_2\) contributes no tangent. Finite coherent amplitude \(E_2^Y\) belongs at amplitude level. It affects \(\ell_7\) only if its local coefficient-map derivative has live \(C_7\)-component.

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(T_{E_2}^{\local})=0
}

unless \(E_2\) explicitly contains local \(C_7\)-renormalization freedom.

A labelled/raw mismatch can contribute to \(\ell_7\) only if the normal-form commutator or realization defect creates the live \(C_7\) carrier. If it is source-linear, chart-linear, whitening-tail, or residual, it should be killed.

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(T_{\lab,Y}^{\local})=0
}
\]

or finite coherent/residual, not local tangent.

If the singular nuisance quotient contains \(e_7\), then \(s_7\) is killed as nuisance. That is not “actual source motion”; it is quotient overcollapse unless structurally justified.

For rank-two, require:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0.
}
\]

\boxed{
\textbf{NoActualS7Motion}_{Y12}.

\ell_7

\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}

\mathcal T_{E_2}^{\local}
+
\mathcal T_{\lab}^{\local}
\right)
=0.
\]

The hard term is

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal T_7^{\arith}).
\]

Everything else has a plausible origin/slot/label vanishing proof.

So the theorem reduces to:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal T_7^{\arith})=0.
}
\]

---

# 5. When can \(\ell_7(\mathcal T_7^{\arith})\neq0\)?

There are only a few possibilities.

## Case A — actual source has a genuine order-seven package coordinate

There is an allowed actual-source family

\[
q''_{\can}(\tau)
\]

such that

\[
\frac{d}{d\tau}q''_{\can}(\tau)\bigg|_{\tau=0}
\]

changes the live \(C_7\) package coefficient.

Then

\[
C_7
\]

is actual-source tangent. Rank two is lost.

## Case B — chart/gauge variation acts on the live \(C_7\) package

A gauge parameter may shift order-seven carrier representatives. If that gauge is actual-source/nuisance, then \(e_7\) enters \(B_Y\).

Need to distinguish:

\[
\text{basis/gauge representation change}
\]

from

\[
\text{actual source deformation}.
\]

If it is merely representation, \(\ell_7\) should kill it or it belongs to nuisance, not actual.

## Case C — \(E_2\) or completion introduces \(C_7\)-renormalization

If completion includes a free local coefficient

\[
\eta_7(p)e_7(p),
\]

then \(s_7\) is locally free. This would collapse rank two, and likely the \(C_7\)-based part of the D4 obstruction.

Expected: no such free local renormalization exists.

## Case D — lower connection is misclassified as actual

A formal lower connection

\[
e_7\mapsto e_7+\rho_Ye_Y
\]

should not by itself count. If the audit includes this formal direction in \(\mathcal T_7^{\arith}\), it will artificially force rank one. This is the key error to avoid.

---

# 6. Operational test

Let

\[
U_7^{\arith}=\{u_j\}
\]

be an actual/nuisance basis for order-seven candidate directions, excluding D4-only formal carriers.

Compute

\[
a_j=\ell_7(u_j).
\]

Then:

- if

\[
a_j=0\quad\forall j,
\]

then \(C_7\) is not actual-source tangent;

- if some

\[
a_j\neq0,
\]

then \(s_7\) is actual-source motion.

If \(s_7\) is actual-source motion, also compute

\[
b_j=\ell_9^Y(u_j).
\]

The one-slope condition is

\[
b_j=\rho_Ya_j
\quad\forall j.
\]

Equivalently,

\[
a_i b_j-a_j b_i=0
\quad\forall i,j.
\]

If it holds, rank one survives.

If not, the \(Y12\) route dies locally.

---

# 7. Consequences for rank two

If

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal T_7^{\arith})=0
\]

and previous vanishings hold, then

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

D4 gives

\[
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Because

\[
c_7\alpha_9\neq0,
\]

this is equivalent to

\[
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]

Then the wall theorem is joint anisotropy:

\[
\boxed{
\int
\min
\left(
Q^A
\frac{|H|^2(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2)}
{\mathcal N_{D4}},
1
\right)
d\nu
\ge
Q^{-B}\nu.
}
\]

This bypasses:

\[
\rho_Y,\quad
\rho_\Gamma,\quad
\text{moving-line finite type},\quad
\text{forced wall ODE}.
\]

# 1. The exact question

}

Equivalently, does the canonical actual-source family have a true local parameter whose infinitesimal effect is the live \(C_7\)-carrier?

If not, then \(C_7\) is D4-only, and the formal \(C_7\to Y12\) lift remains external to the actual-source tangent model.

---

# 2. Actual-source image versus package-coordinate freedom

There are two fundamentally different models.

## Model A — canonical EF image

The actual source is determined by the canonical EF/gamma/tail construction:

\[
q''_{\can}=B_2+P_2+D_2+E_2.
\]

Allowed actual variations are limited to structural parameters:

\[
m,\lambda,\text{phase},\text{normalization},\text{chart/background/gamma},
\]

and possibly fixed global proof-thickening parameters if explicitly promoted to actual-source parameters.

In this model, the image of the actual-source tangent map is source-linear / chart-linear / background-linear. It should not contain arbitrary finite package coordinates. Then:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal T_7^{\arith})=0.
}
\]

This gives \(B_Y=0\), modulo the other already-audited vanishings.

## Model B — free corrected package coordinates

The actual-source model is allowed to vary finite package coordinates, including \(C_7\), as independent local source data.

Then

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal T_7^{\arith})\ne0.
}
\]

If the \(s_9^Y\)-continuation is graph-like, the local fiber model contains

\[
\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y),
\]

and rank two collapses to rank one.

If it has vertical freedom or another slope, the current \(Y12\) route dies locally.

Thus the real audit is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Are finite package coordinates actual source parameters, or merely proof/D4 coordinates?}
}
\]

For the RH obstruction architecture, the safer and sharper model is Model A.

---

# 3. Why canonical EF image should not contain \(C_7\)

A live carrier coordinate like \(C_7\) is a finite local package component. It is not the same as an ordinary order-seven Taylor coefficient of \(q''_{\can}\).

An actual EF derivative may produce order-seven Taylor mass, but \(\ell_7\) is designed to kill non-live/source-linear/background components and read only the live \(C_7\) carrier class.

So the relevant implication is:

\[
\partial_\theta q''_{\can}
\text{ has order-seven Taylor terms}
\centernot\Rightarrow
\ell_7(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})\ne0.
\]

The needed signature match is stricter:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{Origin}(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})
=
\operatorname{Origin}(C_7)
}
\]

in the live carrier quotient.

For ordinary EF/gamma/chart parameters, this should fail.

Therefore:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})=0
}
\]

for all ordinary actual-source parameters.

# 7. Rank-two D4 theorem under \(B_Y=0\)

If

\[
B_Y=0

and the live pair is \(Q^A\)-conditioned, D4 contributes

\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.

The clean coefficient matrix is

=

with

\Gamma_{G,2}

\[

{\mathcal N_{\D4}},

}

No moving-line \(\rho_\Gamma\), no scalar wall finite type, no forced ODE trap.

\exists u,\ \ell_7(u)\ne0;

\quad\text{or}\quad
e_Y\in\mathcal N_{\sing};

If only the first occurs, and all actual \(s_7\)-motions continue with one slope, then rank one remains viable.

---

# 1. Separate two vector spaces that were previously conflated

There are two spaces:

\[
\mathcal I_{\can}
:=
\left\{
\partial_\theta q''_{\can},\ 
\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}q''_{\can},\ldots
\right\},
\]

the image of the actual canonical source family, and

\[
\mathcal P_{\mathrm{pkg}}
:=
\operatorname{span}\{e_7,e_Y,\ldots\},
\]

the finite package carrier space used by D4.

The rank-two route needs:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal I_{\can}\cap \operatorname{span}\{e_7,e_Y\}=0
}
\]

inside the live quotient, up to polynomial conditioning.

More concretely:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal I_{\can})=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal I_{\can})=0.
}
\]

This is the cleanest formulation of \(B_Y=0\).

The dangerous over-quotienting error is to assume

\[
\mathcal P_{\mathrm{pkg}}\subset T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}.
\]

That should be false unless package coordinates are explicitly admitted as actual source parameters.

---

# 2. Canonical EF image is generated by differential operators on fixed atoms

For actual source parameters

\[
\theta\in\{m,\lambda,\text{phase},\text{normalization},\text{chart},\text{background/gamma}\},
\]

the source variation has schematic form

\[
\partial_\theta q''_{\can}
=
\mathcal D_\theta q''_{\can}
+
\text{lower/background correction},
\]

where \(\mathcal D_\theta\) is a differential, multiplication, phase, or coordinate operator acting on the existing EF atoms.

Thus the actual image is closed under operations like

\[
\partial_t,\quad
t\partial_t,\quad
\log n\text{-multipliers},\quad
\text{smooth cutoff derivatives},\quad
\text{gamma/background derivatives}.
\]

These operations do not introduce new finite package carrier origins. They transform the existing canonical source; they do not supply arbitrary coefficients of \(e_7\) or \(e_Y\).

So the structural claim is:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(\mathcal D_\theta q''_{\can})=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal D_\theta q''_{\can})=0
}
\]

because \(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y\) are chosen to detect D4 package carriers, not source-linear EF deformations.

---

# 3. Why order matching is insufficient

A possible objection:

\[
\partial_\theta q''_{\can}
\]

may have order-seven or order-nine Taylor components.

But \(s_7\) and \(s_9^Y\) are not ordinary Taylor coefficients. They are live carrier coordinates after quotienting by source-linear, chart, background, nuisance, and nonprimitive sectors.

So the correct test is not:

\[
[z^7]\partial_\theta q''_{\can}=0.
\]

The correct test is:

\[
\ell_7(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})=0.
\]

This may hold even when the ordinary order-seven coefficient is nonzero, because the nonzero coefficient lies in the killed source-linear/background/chart model.

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
\text{ordinary jet activity does not imply live package activity.}
}
\]

This is the conceptual reason rank two remains plausible.

---

# 4. A useful criterion: annihilation by origin labels

Let

\[
\operatorname{Orig}(v)
\]

denote the refined origin label of a source term.

The live package carrier \(e_7\) has a specific origin label, say

\[
\Sigma(C_7).
\]

The primitive \(Y12\) carrier \(e_Y\) has

\[
\Sigma(Y12)
=
(D^2N_0,D\mathcal W_{\same},12,\mathrm{same},w=1,\operatorname{order}=9,\ldots).
\]

\[
\operatorname{Orig}(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})
\notin
\{\Sigma(C_7),\Sigma(Y12)\},

\[
\ell_7(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})
=
\ell_9^Y(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})

Similarly, if every second derivative satisfies

\operatorname{Orig}(\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}q''_{\can})
\notin
\{\Sigma(C_7),\Sigma(Y12)\},

then actual second curvature contributes nothing to \(B_Y\).

So a practical theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{Orig}(\mathcal I_{\can})
\cap
\{\Sigma(C_7),\Sigma(Y12)\}
=
\varnothing.
}
\]

This is stronger and cleaner than trying to compute every coefficient.

---

# 5. Parameter audit under this criterion

## 5.1 \(m\)-translation

\[
\partial_m
\]

acts as a phase derivative:

\[
e^{-im\log n}\mapsto -i\log n\,e^{-im\log n}.
\]

Origin remains EF-linear.

Therefore:

\[
\ell_7(\partial_mq''_{\can})=0,\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\partial_mq''_{\can})=0.
\]

Second derivatives remain EF-linear:

\[
\ell_7(\partial_m^2q''_{\can})=0,\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\partial_m^2q''_{\can})=0.
\]

## 5.2 \(\lambda\)-scaling

\[
\partial_\lambda
\]

acts on chart scale and local coordinates. If the transition transports formed labelled carriers, this is chart-linear, not live-package creation.

So:

\[
\ell_7(\partial_\lambda q''_{\can})=0,\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\partial_\lambda q''_{\can})=0.
\]

Failure would mean \(\lambda\)-variation recomputes the finite package rather than changing coordinates.

That should be handled by the labelled normal-form commutator.

These are multipliers or proof choices. They are not actual live package coordinates.

Expected:

\[
\ell_7=0,\qquad \ell_9^Y=0.
\]

If a cutoff is allowed to vary freely at extraction scale, it becomes locally free and fatal, but such cutoffs should not be in the actual-source manifold.

## 5.4 Background / gamma

Background/gamma terms lack the mixed nonlinear finite package origin. They should be killed:

\[
\ell_7=0,\qquad \ell_9^Y=0.
\]

## 5.5 \(\alpha\)

If \(\alpha\) is proof-thickening only:

\[
\alpha\notin \Theta_{\arith},
\]

so it contributes nothing to \(B_Y\).

If \(\alpha\) is actual-source internal, one must test its origin. If it only changes phase/weights, it is EF-linear and killed. If it changes \(N_0\), whitening geometry, or finite package coordinates, rank two may fail.

Thus the recommended policy remains:

\[

\alpha\text{ external unless absolutely needed as actual source.}

in the live fiber.

detects whether raw transition recomputes \(N_0\) in a way that generates primitive live package origin.

Prime/dual tails are EF-linear truncation errors:

\[
P_2-P_{2,\le X},\qquad D_2-D_{2,\le X}.
\]

They should satisfy

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\tail}=0
}
\]

by origin filtering. Any nonzero leakage is residual \(R_X^Y\), not local freedom.

## 2.2 \(E_{\reg}\)

Regularization is either proof residual or fixed canonical correction. Unless it explicitly introduces a local primitive \(C_7/Y12\)-package coordinate,

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}T_{E_{\reg}}^{\local}=0.
}
\]

## 2.3 \(E_{\smooth}\)

Smooth remainder is source-linear / background / analytic residual.

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}T_{E_{\smooth}}^{\local}=0.
}
\]

## 2.4 \(E_{\gamma,\mathrm{rem}}\)

Gamma/background remainder has neither live \(C_7\) package origin nor primitive \(Y12\) origin.

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}T_{E_{\gamma,\mathrm{rem}}}^{\local}=0.
}
\]

## 2.5 \(E_{\chart}\)

Chart curvature may have ordinary order-seven or order-nine jet effects, but rank-two fiber detects live package classes, not raw jets. If chart maps transport formed carriers rather than recomputing \(N_0\), then

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}T_{E_{\chart}}^{\local}=0.
}
\]

If chart transition recomputes \(N_0\), the effect belongs to the typed commutator audit, not ordinary \(E_2\).

---

# 3. The only real risk: \(E_{\model}\)

Define two coefficient maps for the live pair:

\[
c_{E,7}(\theta;p):=\ell_7(E_{\model}(\theta;p)),
\]

\[
c_{E,9}(\theta;p):=\ell_9^Y(E_{\model}(\theta;p)).
\]

Rank-two requires that extraction-local derivatives vanish:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}(c_{E,7},c_{E,9})=0,
\qquad
D_{\loc}^2(c_{E,7},c_{E,9})=0.
}
\]

Here \(D_{\loc}\) means local representative freedom at extraction scale, not fixed global variation or fixed coherent basepoint shift.

This distinction matters:

- fixed \(c_E^0(p)\): safe basepoint;
- finite coherent \(c_j(p)\): amplitude model;
- residual \(c_{\res}(p)\): \(R_X^Y\);
- local derivative \(\eta(p)\): enters \(B_Y\), fatal for rank two.

---

# 4. Classification of \(E_{\model}\) outputs

For rank two, each \(E_{\model}^Y\) contribution must be routed into one of the following.

## 4.1 Zero

\[
(c_{E,7},c_{E,9})=0.
\]

Rank-two safe.

## 4.2 Fixed/basepoint

\[
(c_{E,7},c_{E,9})=(c_7^0(p),c_9^0(p))
\]

with no admissible local variation.

Rank-two locally safe. It may shift the model basepoint but does not enter \(B_Y\).

## 4.3 Finite coherent amplitude

\[
(c_{E,7},c_{E,9})
\in
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{E_2}
\]

with finite-dimensional nonlocal/block-coherent span.

Rank-two locally safe, but it creates a vector amplitude-Schur layer:

\[
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\nu)}
(F_{2Y},\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith})^2
\ge Q^{-A}\|F_{2Y}\|^2.
\]

## 4.4 Residual-controlled

Belongs to \(R_X^Y\), requiring targeted \(Y12\)-sector completion:

\[
\sum_\omega
\|\Pi_{\fib,Y}T_{\tr\to\sing}J_{\tr}(R_X^Y)\|_\omega^2
\le Q^{-B}\mu.
\]

Rank-two safe if proven.

## 4.5 Local graph contamination

\[
D_{\loc}(c_{E,7},c_{E,9})
\subset
\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y).
\]

This is safe for rank one but **not** for rank two. It places

\[
\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y)
\]

inside \(B_Y\), collapsing the D4 rank from two to one.

## 4.6 Vertical local contamination

\[
D_{\loc}(c_{E,7},c_{E,9})
\ni (0,1).
\]

Fatal for current \(Y12\) vertical channel.

## 4.7 Second slope / rank-two actual model

Two local derivatives span different slopes. Then \(B_Y\) has rank two and the current \(Y12\) D4 channel is actual-source coherent.

Fatal locally.

---

# 5. Why \(D_{\loc}c_E\) should vanish for canonical \(E_2\)

If \(E_2\) is a canonical completion term determined by EF/gamma/tail/chart data, then it is not an independent local parameter. Its variations under actual source parameters are EF-linear, chart-linear, background, or residual.

They should not create live package coordinates:

\[
e_7,\qquad e_Y.
\]

Thus the expected theorem is

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}(c_{E,7},c_{E,9})=0.
}
\]

Similarly, second derivatives are parameter curvature of EF/chart data, not free live package coordinates:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}^2(c_{E,7},c_{E,9})=0.
}
\]

The only exceptions are:

1. explicit local \(C_7\) or \(Y12\)-renormalization parameters;
2. completion procedure recomputes \(N_0\) and introduces local primitive package terms;
3. \(E_2\) is defined by fitting the actual wall/carrier residual.

Those would kill or weaken rank two.

---

# 6. Finite coherent \(E_2^Y\) is allowed, but moves to vector amplitude Schur

Suppose \(E_2^Y\) gives finite coherent vectors

\[
\phi_a(p)\in\mathbb C^2_{(s_7,s_9^Y)}.
\]

Then define

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{E_2}
=
\operatorname{span}\{\phi_a\}.
\]

Rank-two remains locally valid if this space is not locally free.

Need:

\[
\dim \mathcal C_{2Y,\omega}^{E_2}\le Q^C,
\]

preferably \(O(1)\), plus bounded leverage

\[
\mathfrak L_\omega(p)
\le
Q^C\frac{\dim \mathcal C_{2Y,\omega}^{E_2}}{\nu_\omega(\Omega_\omega)}.
\]

If this holds, \(E_2\) is not part of \(B_Y\), but part of

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

Then the D4 vector wall is tested modulo this coherent model.

---

# 7. The rank-two \(E_2\) theorem

The clean statement is:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{NoLocalCompletionFiberFreedom}_{2Y}.
}
\]

For the completion term \(E_2\),

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}=0.
\]

Equivalently, after decomposing \(E_2\),

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_2
=
E_{2,\fixed}^{2Y}
+
E_{2,\coh}^{2Y}
+
E_{2,\res}^{2Y},
\]

where:

- \(E_{2,\fixed}^{2Y}\) is fixed/basepoint;
- \(E_{2,\coh}^{2Y}\) is finite coherent nonlocal/block-coherent;
- \(E_{2,\res}^{2Y}\) satisfies targeted residual completion;

and no term has local derivative in

\[
\mathbb C^2_{(s_7,s_9^Y)}.
\]

Then \(E_2\) does not contribute to \(B_Y\).

---

# 8. Failure modes

Rank-two fails at the \(E_2\)-layer if:

1. \(E_2\) contains local \(C_7\)-coordinate freedom:

\[
D_{\loc}c_{E,7}\neq0;
\]

2. \(E_2\) contains local primitive \(Y12\)-coordinate freedom:

\[
D_{\loc}c_{E,9}\neq0;
\]

3. \(E_2\) contains graph-compatible local freedom:

\[
D_{\loc}(c_{E,7},c_{E,9})\subset\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y),
\]

which collapses rank two to rank one;

4. \(E_2\) contains a second slope or vertical direction, killing current \(Y12\);

5. \(E_2\) is finite coherent but locally high-leverage / atom-local, causing amplitude-level death.

---

# 9. Current expected outcome

For canonical EF/gamma/tail completion, the favorable expectation is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}=0.
}
\]

Any nonzero \(E_2^Y\) should be one of:

\[
\text{fixed},\quad
\text{finite coherent},\quad
\text{residual-controlled}.
\]

Therefore \(E_2\) should not obstruct \(B_Y=0\).

If this is confirmed alongside canonical image avoidance and labelled commutator safety, rank-two D4 remains the primary route.

## Agent 3 continuation — separating local \(E_2^Y\)-fiber freedom from coherent \(E_2^Y\)-amplitude

I continue with the fixed notation:

\[
\rho_Y
\]

is the \(Y12\)-fiber graph slope,

\[
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9},
\]

and

\[
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.
\]

The rank-two branch requires

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}=0.
\]

It does **not** require \(\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_2^Y=0\) pointwise. The distinction is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{local fiber derivative is forbidden;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{fixed/coherent/residual amplitude is allowed, with later Schur/residual handling.}
}
\]

---

# 1. Local fiber freedom means variation, not presence

Write the two live coordinates of \(E_2\) as

\[
\mathbf c_E(p)
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_{E,7}(p)\\
c_{E,9}(p)
\end{pmatrix}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(E_2(p))\\
\ell_9^Y(E_2(p))
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

The local \(B_Y\)-contribution is not \(\mathbf c_E(p)\) itself. It is the admissible **local variation space**

\[
D_{\local}\mathbf c_E.
\]

Thus:

- if \(\mathbf c_E(p)\) is fixed, it is a basepoint shift;
- if \(\mathbf c_E(p)\) lies in a finite coherent space, it is amplitude-level;
- if \(\mathbf c_E(p)\) is small/tail-like, it is residual;
- only if \(D_{\local}\mathbf c_E\neq0\) does it contribute to \(B_Y\).

So the rank-two condition is exactly

\[
\boxed{
D_{\local}\mathbf c_E=0
}
\]

and, if second-order local freedoms are admitted,

\[
\boxed{
D_{\local}^2\mathbf c_E=0.
}
\]

---

# 2. Canonical \(E_2\) should be a fixed functional, not a local coordinate

A canonical completion term has the schematic form

\[
E_2=E_2[q''_{\can};X,\mathrm{cutoffs},\mathrm{chart}],
\]

where \(X\), cutoffs, and charts are fixed proof choices or globally controlled parameters.

If these are not admissible extraction-local source parameters, then

\[
E_2
\]

does not provide a local coordinate. Its \(Y12\)-live projection may be a fixed function of \(p\), but it is not a tangent direction.

Thus canonical \(E_2\) contributes either:

\[
\mathbf c_E^0(p)
\]

fixed, or

\[
\mathbf c_E^{\mathrm{coh}}(p)
\]

finite coherent/global, or

\[
\mathbf c_E^{\mathrm{res}}(p)
\]

residual.

It does not contribute

\[
\eta(p)
\begin{pmatrix}
1\\0
\end{pmatrix}
\quad\text{or}\quad
\eta(p)
\begin{pmatrix}
0\\1
\end{pmatrix}
\]

with arbitrary local \(\eta(p)\), unless the model explicitly adds such a local renormalization parameter.

Therefore the favorable theorem is natural:

\[
\boxed{
\text{canonical }E_2\text{ has no extraction-local live-fiber degrees of freedom.}
}
\]

---

# 3. Component routing

## 3.1 \(E_{\tail}\)

Tail terms are determined once the truncation scale and cutoff are fixed. If truncation scale is not a local actual-source parameter, then

\[
D_{\local}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\tail}=0.
\]

Any nonzero \(\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\tail}\) is residual:

\[
E_{\tail}^{2Y}\in R_X^Y.
\]

Rank-two safe.

## 3.2 \(E_{\reg}\)

Regularization is fixed by the canonical prescription. It may contribute a fixed/coherent profile, but has no local degree of freedom unless a regularization counterterm is chosen locally.

Safe condition:

\[
D_{\local}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_{\reg}=0.
\]

## 3.3 \(E_{\smooth}\)

Smooth remainder is fixed/residual. No local fiber derivative.

## 3.4 \(E_{\chart}\)

If chart choice is fixed or globally coherent, \(E_{\chart}\) is not local fiber freedom.

Danger only if chart normalization is locally adjusted at extraction scale in a way that changes live \(C_7\) or \(Y12\) coordinates.

That should be excluded from \(T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}\).

## 3.5 \(E_{\gamma,\mathrm{rem}}\)

Gamma remainder is fixed/background. No local live-fiber freedom.

## 3.6 \(E_{\model}\)

Only \(E_{\model}\) can plausibly contain an explicit package-level counterterm.

So the rank-two audit reduces to:

\[
\boxed{
E_{\model}\text{ has no local }C_7\text{ or }Y12\text{ counterterm.}
}
\]

Equivalently, no local parameters

\[
\eta_7(p),\eta_Y(p)
\]

with

\[
E_{\model}^{2Y}(p)
=
\eta_7(p)e_7(p)+\eta_Y(p)e_Y(p).
\]

If such parameters exist, rank two fails.

---

# 4. Graph-compatible \(E_2^Y\) is still not rank-two compatible

If

\[
D_{\local}\mathbf c_E
\subset
\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y),
\]

then this is graph-compatible for rank one. But it still means

\[
B_Y\supset \mathbb C(1,\rho_Y),
\]

so rank two is lost.

Thus for rank two we need the stronger condition:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\local}\mathbf c_E=0,
}
\]

not merely one-slope.

This is important. Earlier “safe” graph terms are safe only for the rank-one fallback.

---

# 5. Finite coherent \(E_2\) in the rank-two route

If \(E_2\) contributes finite coherent vector amplitudes

\[
\phi_a(p)\in\mathbb C^2,
\]

define

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{E_2}
=
\operatorname{span}\{\phi_a\}.
\]

Then the D4 vector wall is

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

The amplitude-Schur layer becomes vector-valued:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\nu)}
(F_{2Y},\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith})^2
\ge
Q^{-A}\|F_{2Y}\|^2.
}
\]

This is acceptable if \(\mathcal C_{2Y}^{E_2}\) is finite-dimensional nonlocal with bounded leverage. It is not a local \(B_Y\)-contribution.

So rank-two allows:

\[
E_2^Y\neq0
\]

as long as it is fixed/coherent/residual rather than local.

---

# 6. The exact no-local-completion theorem

A precise theorem:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{NoLocalCompletionFiberFreedom}_{2Y}.
}
\]

Let

\[
\mathbf c_E(p)
=
\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_2(p).
\]

Assume:

1. all completion choices \(X,\) cutoffs, regularization, charts, and tail decompositions are fixed before extraction or vary only in finite-dimensional global/block-coherent families;

2. no explicit local live-package counterterms \(\eta_7(p)e_7+\eta_Y(p)e_Y\) are admitted;

3. chart/transition operations transport formed typed carriers rather than recomputing local \(N_0\)-package coordinates;

4. any nonzero \(\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_2\) is decomposed as fixed \(+\) finite coherent \(+\) residual.

Then

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}=0.
}
\]

Consequently \(E_2\) does not contribute to \(B_Y\).

---

# 7. What would disprove it

The theorem fails if there exists any extraction-local parameter satisfying one of:

\[
\partial_{\eta}\mathbf c_E
=
\begin{pmatrix}
1\\0
\end{pmatrix},
\]

\[
\partial_{\eta}\mathbf c_E
=
\begin{pmatrix}
0\\1
\end{pmatrix},
\]

\[
\partial_{\eta}\mathbf c_E
=
\begin{pmatrix}
1\\ \rho_Y
\end{pmatrix},
\]

or any second independent vector.

Interpretation:

- \((1,0)\): local \(C_7\)-completion coordinate; rank two fails, possibly rank one depending on continuation.
- \((0,1)\): local \(Y12\)-vertical coordinate; current route dies.
- \((1,\rho_Y)\): graph local coordinate; rank two collapses to rank one.
- two independent vectors: local actual model absorbs the live fiber; route dies.

Thus the finite test is:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{rank}D_{\local}\mathbf c_E=0
}
\]

for rank two.

---

# 8. Current expectation

For canonical \(E_2\), the expected output is:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\local}\mathbf c_E=0.
}
\]

Any nonzero \(E_2^{2Y}\) should be:

\[
\text{fixed},\quad
\text{finite coherent},\quad
\text{or residual-controlled}.
\]

Therefore \(E_2\) should not obstruct \(B_Y=0\).

The main remaining local rank-two threats are then:

1. actual \(C_7\)-motion from the canonical source family;
2. local labelled-transition commutator fiber defect;
3. nuisance quotient touching the live pair.

But \(E_2\) itself should be safe if no local completion counterterms are admitted.

## Agent 3 continuation — distinguishing fixed \(E_2^{2Y}\) from hidden local renormalization

I continue with the fixed notation:

\[
\rho_Y=\text{the }Y12\text{-fiber graph slope},
\qquad
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9},
\qquad
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.
\]

The rank-two target is still

\[
B_Y=0,
\]

so for \(E_2\) we need the stronger condition

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}=0,
\]

not merely graph compatibility. A graph-compatible local \(E_2^Y\)-direction would already put \(\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y)\) into \(B_Y\), collapsing rank two to rank one. The latest prompt’s correction is precisely that rank two is live only if local actual-source/completion freedoms do not touch the live fiber at all. 

---

# 1. Hidden local renormalization is the only serious \(E_2\) threat

Let

\[
\mathbf c_E(p)
=
\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_2(p)
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_{E,7}(p)\\
c_{E,9}(p)
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

A fixed nonzero value

\[
\mathbf c_E^0(p)
\]

does not enter \(B_Y\). A finite-dimensional coherent family

\[
\mathbf c_E(p)=\sum_{a=1}^{M}u_a\phi_a(p)
\]

does not enter \(B_Y\) either, provided the coefficients \(u_a\) are global/block-coherent and fixed before extraction. It enters the vector amplitude model

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

The dangerous case is a local renormalization of the form

\[
\mathbf c_E(p;\eta)
=
\mathbf c_E^0(p)+
\eta_7(p)
\begin{pmatrix}1\\0\end{pmatrix}
+
\eta_9(p)
\begin{pmatrix}0\\1\end{pmatrix},
\]

or even

\[
\mathbf c_E(p;\eta)
=
\mathbf c_E^0(p)+
\eta(p)
\begin{pmatrix}1\\ \rho_Y\end{pmatrix}.
\]

The first kills the \(Y12\) route locally; the second collapses rank two to rank one. Therefore the sharp theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\local}\mathbf c_E=0.
}
\]

---

# 2. A precise “local” criterion

The word “local” should be formalized by extraction-scale degrees of freedom.

Let \(\{\Omega_\omega\}\) be the pre-extraction block decomposition, and let each block contain \(N_\omega\) candidate atoms or microcells.

A completion family is local if its restriction map has rank comparable to the number of atoms:

\[
\operatorname{rank}
\left(
D\mathbf c_E|_{\Omega_\omega}
\right)
\asymp N_\omega,
\]

with polynomially conditioned interpolation.

A completion family is coherent if

\[
\operatorname{rank}
\left(
D\mathbf c_E|_{\Omega_\omega}
\right)
\le Q^C,
\]

preferably \(O(1)\), and has bounded leverage.

So the no-local-completion condition can be stated as:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{rank}_{\mathrm{atom}}
D_{\local}\mathbf c_E=0,
}
\]

or, in the weaker amplitude-compatible form,

\[
\boxed{
D\mathbf c_E(\Theta_{\mathrm{coh}})
\subset \mathcal C_{2Y}^{E_2}
\quad\text{with low dimension and bounded leverage.}
}
\]

For rank two, any genuinely local derivative in \(\mathbb C^2_{(s_7,s_9^Y)}\) is forbidden.

---

# 3. Why canonical completion should be nonlocal

Canonical \(E_2\) terms are normally determined by global or semi-global rules:

\[
E_2
=
E_{\tail}
+
E_{\reg}
+
E_{\smooth}
+
E_{\chart}
+
E_{\gamma,\mathrm{rem}}
+
E_{\model}.
\]

The choices involved are things like:

\[
X,\quad \text{cutoff shape},\quad \text{regularization scheme},\quad \text{chart scale},\quad \text{tail split}.
\]

These are proof choices or fixed canonical choices. They are not extraction-local source coordinates.

Thus even if these choices change \(E_2\), their induced functions

\[
\partial_X\mathbf c_E,\quad
\partial_{\mathrm{cutoff}}\mathbf c_E,\quad
\partial_{\mathrm{chart}}\mathbf c_E
\]

are coherent profiles, not atom-local degrees of freedom. If admitted at all, they belong to

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{E_2}.
\]

They do not belong to

\[
B_Y.
\]

So canonical completion should satisfy:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}=0
}
\]

by construction, unless the model explicitly adds local counterterms.

---

# 4. The explicit local-counterterm no-go

The rank-two route fails at \(E_2\) if the canonical model includes local counterterms

\[
E_{\model}^{2Y}
=
\eta_7(p)e_7(p)+\eta_9(p)e_Y(p),
\]

with \(\eta_7,\eta_9\) adjustable at extraction scale.

Then

\[
D_{\local}\mathbf c_E
=
\mathbb C^2,
\]

so

\[
B_Y=\mathbb C^2,
\]

and the current \(Y12\) route dies locally.

If only

\[
E_{\model}^{2Y}
=
\eta(p)(e_7+\rho_Ye_Y),
\]

then

\[
B_Y=\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y),
\]

so rank two collapses to the rank-one moving-wall route.

Thus the no-go classification is exact:

\[
\begin{array}{c|c}
D_{\local}\mathbf c_E & \text{effect}\\
\hline
0 & \text{rank-two compatible}\\
\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y) & \text{rank-one fallback}\\
\mathbb C(0,1) & \text{vertical }Y12\text{ death}\\
\mathbb C^2 & \text{live fiber locally absorbed}
\end{array}
\]

---

# 5. The finite coherent case under rank two

Suppose

\[
\mathbf c_E(p)
=
\sum_{a=1}^{M} u_a \phi_a(p),
\qquad
\phi_a(p)\in\mathbb C^2.
\]

Then define

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{E_2}
=
\operatorname{span}\{\phi_a\}.
\]

Rank two is still locally intact if \(\{u_a\}\) are not extraction-local. The downstream lower becomes vector amplitude-Schur:

\[
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\nu)}
\left(
F_{2Y},
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
\right)^2
\ge
Q^{-A}\|F_{2Y}\|^2,
\]

where

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

This is often still easier than the rank-one scalar moving wall, because \(F_{2Y}\) has two components and no moving-line null direction.

---

# 6. \(E_{\chart}\) and \(E_{\model}\) should be separated

A subtlety: some chart effects were listed inside \(E_2\), but chart effects should be split into:

## Chart representation effects

These are changes in coordinates or basis. They belong to the denominator/model quotient and should be killed by \(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y\).

Rank-two safe.

## Chart recomputation effects

These recompute \(N_0\) or same-point whitening after changing the chart. These are not ordinary \(E_2\); they are normal-form commutator effects.

They should be routed to

\[
\mathfrak C_Y.
\]

So in the \(E_2\)-audit, \(E_{\chart}\) should be considered safe only after verifying it is representational, not recomputational.

This prevents double-counting the same defect in both \(E_2\) and labelled transition.

---

# 7. Strong theorem statement

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{NoLocalCompletionFiberFreedom}_{2Y}.
}
\]

Let

\[
\mathbf c_E(p)=\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_2(p).
\]

Assume:

1. \(E_2\) is canonically determined by EF/gamma/tail/chart data, with no extraction-local live-package counterterms;

2. chart effects in \(E_2\) are representational, while recomputation defects are routed to the typed commutator audit;

3. any nonzero \(\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_2\) is fixed, finite-dimensional coherent with bounded leverage, or residual-controlled;

4. no admitted \(E_2\)-parameter \(\eta\) satisfies

\[
\partial_\eta\mathbf c_E\in
\mathbb C^2_{(s_7,s_9^Y)}
\]

at extraction scale.

Then

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}=0.
}
\]

Thus \(E_2\) does not contribute to \(B_Y\).

---

# 8. Residual form needed for completion

After removing fixed/coherent pieces, define

\[
R_{X,E_2}^{2Y}
=
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left(

# 1. The rank-two \(E_2\)-condition

=

c_E^{\coh}

This is the exact difference between a completion term and a local counterterm.

E_2

is determined by the canonical source construction. There are no free local coefficients.

and

}

Everything else is secondary.

Tail is determined by cutoff and truncation.

If cutoff/truncation are fixed globally or dyadically before extraction,

\[
D_{\local}E_{\tail}=0.

If the cutoff/truncation is adjusted per extraction atom to fit the row, then it is locally free and invalid.

R_{E_{\tail}}^{2Y}.

or similar.

=
\operatorname{span}\{\phi_1,\ldots,\phi_M\},
\qquad

\le
Q^C\frac{M_\omega}{\nu(\Omega_\omega)}

for an orthonormal basis \(e_j\).

\in

+
R_X^{2Y}
+

with \(E_2\) absent from the local tangent part.

The last step showed that canonical \(E_2\) is rank-two-compatible if it is a functorial completion term, not an extraction-local live-package counterterm. I now make the remaining test completely explicit.

---

# 1. The exact binary fork

The \(E_2\)-layer has only one local fork:

\[
\boxed{
E_2\text{ is functorial/canonical}
}
\]

or

\[
\boxed{
E_2\text{ contains local live-fiber renormalization.}
}
\]

In formulas, write

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_2(p)
=
\mathbf c_E(p)
=
(c_{E,7}(p),c_{E,9}(p)).
\]

The relevant local tangent is

\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\local}\mathbf c_E=0
\Rightarrow
E_2\text{ contributes nothing to }B_Y.
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
D_{\local}\mathbf c_E\neq0
\Rightarrow
E_2\text{ changes the local rank fork.}
}
\]

This is independent of whether

\[
\mathbf c_E(p)
\]

itself is nonzero.

---

# 2. Local directions and their consequences

The possible nonzero local images are exactly:

## 2.1 Graph local image

\[
D_{\local}\mathbf c_E
=
\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y).
\]

Then

\[
B_Y\supseteq\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y).
\]

Rank two collapses to rank one. This is not fatal for the \(Y12\) route, but it activates the moving-wall fallback.

## 2.2 Vertical local image

\[
D_{\local}\mathbf c_E
=
\mathbb C(0,1).
\]

Then primitive \(Y12\) is actual-source coherent. The rank-one \(Y12\) vertical channel dies.

## 2.3 Horizontal local image

\[
D_{\local}\mathbf c_E
=
\mathbb C(1,0).
\]

This gives actual \(s_7\)-motion. Unless the continuation is graph-corrected elsewhere, the rank-two route collapses. If combined with a \(Y12\) graph continuation, it becomes the rank-one case.

## 2.4 Two-dimensional local image

\[
D_{\local}\mathbf c_E=\mathbb C^2.
\]

The live fiber is locally absorbed. Current \(Y12\) D4 route dies.

Thus rank two requires the strict zero condition, not merely “no vertical.”

---

# 3. What \(E_2\) may still contribute safely

The following are all compatible with rank two:

## 3.1 Fixed basepoint

\[
\mathbf c_E(p)=\mathbf c_0(p)
\]

with no admissible variation.

This shifts the reference row but does not enter \(B_Y\).

## 3.2 Finite coherent vector amplitude

\[
\mathbf c_E(p)=\sum_{a=1}^M u_a\phi_a(p)
\]

with \(u_a\) global or block-coherent and fixed before extraction.

This enters

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{E_2}
\]

and is handled by vector amplitude Schur.

## 3.3 Residual

\[
\mathbf c_E^{\res}
\]

with

\[
\sum_\omega\|\mathbf c_E^{\res}\|^2_{\Omega_\omega}
\le Q^{-B}\mu.
\]

This enters vector \(\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y}\).

None of these enters the local fiber model

\[
B_Y.
\]

---

# 4. Practical audit checklist

To certify the \(E_2\)-layer, check the following.

## Check 1 — no local counterterms in \(E_{\model}\)

\[

such that

=

## Check 2 — tail and regularization fixed pre-extraction

No atomwise tuning.

## Check 3 — chart recomputation routed away

Chart recomputation of \(N_0\), \(D^2N_0\), or same-point whitening must be assigned to

\[
\mathrm{TypedCommutator}_{2Y},

not hidden inside \(E_2\).
