## Agent 3 — \(s_9^Y\)-row: fixed-\(\alpha\) slot exactness

I will use the existing nomenclature:

\[
\rho_Y
\]

is the \(Y12\)-fiber graph slope,

\[
s_9^Y=\rho_Ys_7+\eta_{\mathrm{fixed}}.
\]

\[
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}
\]

is the rank-one fallback wall ratio.

\[
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}
\]

is the O2/O3 fold ratio. I will not use \(r\) for these.

The assigned task is to prove or falsify

\[
Y12RowExactness^{\fixed\alpha}.
\]

The preferred route is the same-point whitening exactness test:

\[
P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}\subseteq W_{\dead}^{\fixed},
\qquad
a_Y\notin_{Q^{-A}}W_{\dead}^{\fixed}.
\]

This is a local exactness theorem only. The honest D4 wall, if \(B_Y=0\), is the plain rank-two wall

\[
\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\},
\]

not the jet family. Derivatives of \(\Gamma_G\) are analytic auxiliaries unless a derivative-D4-channel theorem is separately proved. 

---

# 1. Primitive \(Y12\)-row signature

The primitive \(Y12\) row is the typed carrier

\[
DW_{\same}[a_Y]\,D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2],
\]

with signature

\[
\boxed{
(D^2N_0,\ DW_{\same},\ \mathrm{mixed}\ 12,\ \mathrm{same\ point},\ w=1,\ [C_{Y12}]).
}
\]

This signature has three live slots:

\[
a_Y\in W,
\qquad
\xi_1\in S_1,
\qquad
\xi_2\in S_2.
\]

Actual fixed-\(\alpha\) source curvature can produce the \(Y12\)-row only if it supplies all three live slots, modulo the dead typed denominators.

The most efficient way to kill the row is to kill it at the whitening slot.

---

# 2. Whitening slot space and denominator

Let \(W\) be the finite whitening input space visible to the order-nine singular extractor.

Let

\[
a_Y\in W
\]

be the live same-point whitening input.

Define

\[
W_{\dead}^{\fixed}
=
W_{\global}
+
W_{\chart}
+
W_{\norm}
+
W_{\bg}
+
W_{\transported}
+
W_{\wrongorigin}.
\]

Here:

- \(W_{\global}\) contains fixed global frame/whitening choices.
- \(W_{\chart}\) contains chart/scale/coordinate whitening drift.
- \(W_{\norm}\) contains normalization/gauge whitening drift.
- \(W_{\bg}\) contains gamma/background whitening effects.
- \(W_{\transported}\) contains transported formed-source whitening.
- \(W_{\wrongorigin}\) contains terms with the wrong nonlinear origin, such as

\[
DW[\eta]DN_0[\zeta],
\]

\[
D^2W[\eta_1,\eta_2]N_0,
\]

\[
DW[\eta]E_2,
\]

\[
DW[\eta](\mathrm{EF\text{-}linear}).
\]

The quotient is

\[
\mathcal Q_W^Y=W/W_{\dead}^{\fixed}.
\]

The exactness target is

\[
\boxed{
[P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}]=0,
\qquad
[a_Y]\neq 0
\quad
\text{in }\mathcal Q_W^Y.
}
\]

---

# 3. Actual fixed-\(\alpha\) whitening image

For fixed-\(\alpha\), the actual variation space is generated by

\[
m,\lambda,\text{phase},\text{chart},\text{normalization},\text{gamma/background}.
\]

Let

\[
V_{\arith}^{\fixed}
\]

be the span of these fixed-target actual first variations.

Let

\[
P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}
\]

be their projection to the whitening slot.

The expected placements are:

\[
P_W(\partial_m)\in W_{\wrongorigin}+W_{\transported},
\]

because \(m\)-translation differentiates EF phases, not same-point \(DW_{\same}[D^2N_0]\).

\[
P_W(\partial_\lambda)\in W_{\chart}+W_{\transported},
\]

because \(\lambda\)-scaling changes chart/frame data.

\[
P_W(\partial_{\mathrm{phase}})\in W_{\wrongorigin},
\]

because phase variation is EF-linear.

\[
P_W(\partial_{\mathrm{norm}})\in W_{\norm}.
\]

\[
P_W(\partial_{\gamma/\bg})\in W_{\bg}.
\]

Thus the desired inclusion is

\[
\boxed{
P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}\subseteq W_{\dead}^{\fixed}.
}
\]

---

# 4. Live same-point whitening survival

The live vector \(a_Y\) must not be a chart, transported, normalization, background, or wrong-origin whitening input.

The quantitative condition is

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}(a_Y,W_{\dead}^{\fixed})
\ge Q^{-A}\|a_Y\|.
}
\]

This condition has two layers.

First, \(a_Y\) must have true same-point support:

\[
a_Y\notin_{Q^{-A}}
W_{\global}+W_{\chart}+W_{\norm}+W_{\bg}+W_{\transported}.
\]

Second, \(a_Y\) must have true \(D^2N_0\)-origin:

\[
DW_{\same}[a_Y]D^2N_0
\notin
\operatorname{span}
\{DW_{\same}DN_0,\ D^2W_{\same}N_0,\ DW_{\same}E_2,\ DW_{\same}\mathrm{EF\text{-}lin}\}.
\]

If either fails, that is a typed quotient/candidate-carrier failure, not immediately an actual-source absorption result.

---

# 5. Finite Schur/covector test

Choose coordinates in \(W\).

Let \(D_W\) span \(W_{\dead}^{\fixed}\).

Let \(M_W\) span \(P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}\).

The finite Schur test is

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{ran}M_W\subseteq\operatorname{ran}D_W,
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}(a_Y,\operatorname{ran}D_W)
\ge Q^{-A}\|a_Y\|.
}
\]

Equivalently,

\[
P_{D_W^\perp}M_W=0,
\]

and

\[
\|P_{D_W^\perp}a_Y\|
\ge Q^{-A}\|a_Y\|.
\]

Then define

\[
a_Y^\perp=P_{D_W^\perp}a_Y,
\]

\[
\omega_Y(w)
=
\frac{\langle w,a_Y^\perp\rangle}
{\langle a_Y,a_Y^\perp\rangle}.
\]

This gives

\[
\omega_Y(a_Y)=1,
\]

\[
\omega_Y(P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed})=0,
\]

and

\[
\|\omega_Y\|\le Q^A\|a_Y\|^{-1}.
\]

This is the desired bounded live row covector for the \(Y12\) whitening slot.

---

# 6. Consequence for actual second curvature

A dangerous actual second-curvature term has the form

\[
DW_{\same}[v_W]D^2N_0[v_1,v_2],
\]

where

\[
v_W\in P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}.
\]

If SamePointWhiteningExactness holds, then

\[
\omega_Y(v_W)=0.
\]

So actual fixed-\(\alpha\) second curvature is killed before we need to inspect \(v_1,v_2\).

Therefore,

\[
\boxed{
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0.
}
\]

First actual tangents are also killed because they do not have the full primitive \(DW_{\same}[D^2N_0]\) typed structure.

Thus

\[
\boxed{
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0
}
\]

for fixed-\(\alpha\) actual-source variations, provided the whitening test passes.

---

# 7. Fallback cascade if whitening fails

If whitening exactness fails, rank two is not automatically dead. Use the slot cascade.

## Level 1 — source slot 1

Define \(S_1\), dead subspace \(S_{1,\dead}^{\fixed}\), actual image \(P_1V_{\arith}^{\fixed}\), and live vector \(\xi_1^Y\).

If

\[
P_1V_{\arith}^{\fixed}\subseteq S_{1,\dead}^{\fixed},
\]

and

\[
\xi_1^Y\notin_{Q^{-A}}S_{1,\dead}^{\fixed},
\]

then the \(Y12\)-row is still killed.

## Level 2 — source slot 2

Similarly, if

\[
P_2V_{\arith}^{\fixed}\subseteq S_{2,\dead}^{\fixed},
\]

and

\[
\xi_2^Y\notin_{Q^{-A}}S_{2,\dead}^{\fixed},
\]

then the row is killed.

## Level 3 — coupled bilinear image

If all individual slots are inconclusive, test

\[
b_Y^{\live}
=
DW_{\same}[a_Y]D^2N_0[\xi_1^Y,\xi_2^Y].
\]

Let

\[
\mathcal B_{\arith}^{\fixed}
=
\operatorname{span}
\{
DW_{\same}[v_W]D^2N_0[v_1,v_2]
:
v_W\in V_W,\ v_i\in V_i
\}.
\]

Let \(\mathcal H_{\dead}^{Y12}\) be the dead subspace of wrong-origin, wrong-branch, wrong-support, transported, and wrong-whitening-count terms.

The final test is

\[
\operatorname{dist}
\left(
b_Y^{\live},
\mathcal B_{\arith}^{\fixed}
+
\mathcal H_{\dead}^{Y12}
\right)
\ge Q^{-A}\|b_Y^{\live}\|.
\]

If this holds, then

\[
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.
\]

If it fails, fixed-\(\alpha\) actual curvature genuinely reaches the primitive \(Y12\) row and must be classified in \(B_Y\).

---

# 8. Failure classification

If the coupled image reaches the live row, compute live fiber coordinates of the surviving actual-curvature columns:

\[
u_j\mapsto
(a_j,b_j)
=
(\ell_7(u_j),\ell_9^Y(u_j)).
\]

Then:

- If all \((a_j,b_j)=0\), the apparent coupled failure is killed elsewhere.
- If all nonzero columns lie in \(\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y)\), rank two collapses to rank one.
- If some column lies in \(\mathbb C(0,1)\), primitive \(Y12\) is actual-source coherent and the current \(Y12\) route dies.
- If two columns have nonzero determinant

\[
a_i b_j-a_j b_i\neq0,
\]

then the live fiber is absorbed by actual curvature and the current route dies locally.

---

# 9. The \(\alpha\)-fork

If \(\alpha\) is proof-thickening, it remains outside

\[
V_{\arith}^{\fixed}.
\]

If \(\alpha\) is internal actual-source motion, add \(P_W(\partial_\alpha)\) to \(M_W\) and rerun the cascade.

If \(\partial_\alpha\) supplies \(a_Y\), then fixed-\(\alpha\) exactness no longer applies, and the induced \(B_Y\)-column must be classified.

Thus for the rank-two fixed-target theorem:

\[
\boxed{
\alpha\notin T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
}
\]

unless explicitly audited.

---

# 10. Final theorem statement

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{Y12RowExactness}^{\fixed\alpha}.
}
\]

For fixed-\(\alpha\) actual-source variations, if at least one of the following holds:

1. same-point whitening exactness;
2. source-slot 1 exactness;
3. source-slot 2 exactness;
4. coupled bilinear image exactness;

then

\[
\boxed{
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.
}
\]

The preferred route is same-point whitening exactness:

\[
\boxed{
P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}\subseteq W_{\dead}^{\fixed},
\qquad
a_Y\notin_{Q^{-A}}W_{\dead}^{\fixed}.
}
\]

## Agent 3 continuation — reducing the \(Y12\)-row to one falsifiable whitening naturality claim

I continue with the fixed notation:

\[
\rho_Y=\text{the }Y12\text{-fiber graph slope},\qquad
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9},\qquad
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.
\]

The current theorem is

\[
Y12RowExactness^{\fixed\alpha}
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.
\]

The preferred proof is still same-point whitening exactness:

\[
P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}\subseteq W_{\dead}^{\fixed},
\qquad
a_Y\notin_{Q^{-A}}W_{\dead}^{\fixed}.
\]

I now reduce this to one sharper claim: **fixed-\(\alpha\) canonical variations transport whitening; they do not recompute the primitive same-point whitening slot.** This is the \(Y12\)-row analogue of the \(C_7\)-row covariant determinant guardrail. 

---

# 1. The single dangerous operation

Every fixed-\(\alpha\) actual variation is safe if it acts by transport:

\[
W_{\same,\tr}\longmapsto S_W W_{\same,\tr} S^{-1}
\]

or by dead chart/gauge/background modifications.

It becomes dangerous only if the variation changes the primitive slot itself:

\[
D_{\theta}W_{\same}
\quad\text{has a component along}\quad
a_Y
\]

inside the quotient

\[
\mathcal Q_W^Y=W/W_{\dead}^{\fixed}.
\]

So the core claim is:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{TypedWhiteningNaturality}_{Y}^{\fixed\alpha}:
\qquad
P_{D_W^\perp}P_W(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})=0
\quad
\forall\theta\in\Theta_{\arith}^{\fixed}.
}
\]

This is exactly the matrix condition

\[
P_{D_W^\perp}M_W=0.
\]

Thus the \(Y12\)-row does not require many separate computations if we can prove whitening naturality.

---

# 2. What whitening naturality must kill

For each fixed actual parameter \(\theta\), we need to show its whitening contribution is one of the following dead types:

\[
W_{\global},\quad W_{\chart},\quad W_{\norm},\quad W_{\bg},\quad W_{\transported},\quad W_{\wrongorigin}.
\]

Concretely:

\[
\partial_m\quad\Rightarrow\quad\text{EF phase / transported};
\]

\[
\partial_\lambda,\partial_{\chart}\quad\Rightarrow\quad\text{chart / transported};
\]

\[
\partial_{\mathrm{norm}}\quad\Rightarrow\quad\text{normalization};
\]

\[
\partial_{\gamma/\bg}\quad\Rightarrow\quad\text{background};
\]

\[
\partial_{\mathrm{phase}}\quad\Rightarrow\quad\text{EF-linear / wrong origin}.
\]

None of these should produce the live type

\[
DW_{\same}[a_Y]D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2].
\]

The precise annihilation condition is:

\[
\omega_Y(P_W\partial_\theta)=0.
\]

---

# 3. Live survival of \(a_Y\)

The second half is independent:

\[
P_{D_W^\perp}a_Y\neq0.
\]

Equivalently, \(a_Y\) is not transported/chart/gauge/background/wrong-origin.

The strongest formulation is typed:

\[
a_Y\in W_{\same}^{D^2N_0,\mathrm{mixed}\,12,w=1},
\]

while

\[
W_{\dead}^{\fixed}
\subset
W_{\transported}
+
W_{\chart}
+
W_{\norm}
+
W_{\bg}
+
W_{\same}^{DN_0}
+
W_{\same}^{N_0}
+
W_{\same}^{E_2}
+
W_{\same}^{\mathrm{EF\text{-}linear}}.
\]

So the desired separation is:

\[
\boxed{
W_{\same}^{D^2N_0,\mathrm{mixed}\,12,w=1}
\cap
W_{\dead}^{\fixed}
=0
}
\]

with polynomial angle.

This is a typed quotient faithfulness statement, not a numerical coincidence.

---

# 4. If chart variation is the spoiler

The most plausible spoiler is chart or scale variation:

\[
\partial_\lambda,\quad \partial_{\chart}.
\]

These can move same-point coordinates. The safe condition is that their effect is **covariant transport** of the formed whitening slot, not a recomputation of \(a_Y\).

So the key chart identity is:

\[
D_{\theta}W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_W(D_{\theta}W_{\tr,\same})S^{-1}
\in W_{\chart}+W_{\transported}+W_{\wrongorigin}.
\]

Applying the live covector,

\[
\omega_Y\left(
D_{\theta}W_{\sing,\same}
-
S_W(D_{\theta}W_{\tr,\same})S^{-1}
\right)=0.
\]

If this fails, the chart is atomically changing the live same-point whitening input. That is not a fixed-source curvature issue; it is a typed transition/commutator defect.

Thus chart failure routes to

\[
\mathrm{TypedCommutator}_{2Y},
\]

not directly to rank-one.

---

# 5. Source-slot fallback becomes unnecessary if whitening naturality passes

Once

\[
\omega_Y(P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed})=0,
\]

any actual second-curvature term

\[
DW_{\same}[v_W]D^2N_0[v_1,v_2]
\]

has zero \(Y12\)-row because

\[
\omega_Y(v_W)=0.
\]

Therefore the source slots are irrelevant in the passing case.

The full source-slot cascade is only needed if

\[
P_{D_W^\perp}M_W\neq0.
\]

So the decision order is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{prove whitening naturality first; do not spend effort on }D^2N_0\text{ slots unless it fails.}
}
\]

---

# 6. A compact finite theorem

The whole \(s_9^Y\)-row can now be stated as:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{WhiteningRowSchur}_{Y}^{\fixed\alpha}.
}
\]

Let \(D_W\) span \(W_{\dead}^{\fixed}\), let \(M_W\) span fixed-\(\alpha\) actual whitening variations, and let \(a_Y\) be the live same-point whitening input. If

\[
\operatorname{ran}M_W\subseteq\operatorname{ran}D_W
\]

and

\[
\operatorname{dist}(a_Y,\operatorname{ran}D_W)
\ge Q^{-A}\|a_Y\|,
\]

then there exists a bounded covector \(\omega_Y\) with

\[
\omega_Y(a_Y)=1,\qquad \omega_Y(M_W)=0.
\]

Consequently,

\[
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.
\]

This proves the \(s_9^Y\)-row exactness for fixed-\(\alpha\).

---

# 7. Failure taxonomy

The possible failures are now exact.

## Failure A — live \(a_Y\) killed

\[
a_Y\in W_{\dead}^{\fixed}.
\]

This is quotient overcollapse or a bad \(Y12\) carrier. It attacks live-pair survival, not actual-source exactness.

## Failure B — fixed actual whitening accesses \(a_Y\)

\[
P_{D_W^\perp}M_W\neq0.
\]

Then actual variations supply the live same-point whitening slot. Continue to source-slot and coupled-image tests.

## Failure C — chart recomputation

Only chart/scale variables produce the nonzero live component. Then the defect belongs to \(\mathrm{TypedCommutator}_{2Y}\). Repair by making transition functorial or by routing the defect.

## Failure D — \(\alpha\)-internality

Only \(\partial_\alpha\) accesses \(a_Y\). Then fixed-\(\alpha\) theorem still holds if \(\alpha\) is proof-external. If \(\alpha\) is actual-source internal, rank-two must be re-audited.

---

# 8. Rank-two implication

If WhiteningRowSchur passes, then

\[
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal T_{\par}^{\actual}+\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0.
\]

Combined with the \(s_7\)-row theorem

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0,
\]

we get

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}=0.
\]

Then \(B_Y=0\) depends only on:

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(E_2+R_{\lab})=0
\]

and live-pair/nuisance preservation.

Thus, after this row closes, the remaining rank-two exactness blockers are no-atom-fitting and nuisance overcollapse.

---

# 9. Current endpoint

The \(Y12\)-row should now be attacked as a finite typed Schur complement in the whitening space:

\[
\boxed{
P_{D_W^\perp}M_W=0,
\qquad
P_{D_W^\perp}a_Y\neq0.
}
\]

The only serious fixed-\(\alpha\) spoiler is chart/whitening recomputation masquerading as same-point \(a_Y\). If transition is functorial and \(a_Y\) survives the typed quotient, the \(s_9^Y\)-row is closed.

:=

-

for fixed-\(\alpha\) actual chart/scale variations.

=

as operators.

This allows:

- transported frame drift;
- normalization/gauge drift;
- wrong-origin same-point terms;
- finite coherent nonlocal terms;
- residual-small defects.

It forbids only a live same-point \(a_Y\)-component controlled by actual fixed-source variation.

\mathfrak W_Y[D^2N_0]\in W_{\chart}+W_{\transported}.

with fixed/block-coherent coefficients.

For fixed actual variations,

V_{\arith}^{\fixed}

can only contribute through:

1. direct whitening slot variation;
2. normal-form transport defect;
3. same-point whitening transport defect;
4. wrong-origin tail/completion terms.

at the local-fiber level.

## 7.4 Normal-form defect accesses \(Y12\)

\]

or

\[
P_{D_W^\perp}\mathfrak C_Y^L\neq0.
\]

Again: fixed/coherent/residual is allowed; atom-local live-fiber derivative is not.

\omega_Y(P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed})=0.
\]

---

# 3. Fixed-\(\alpha\) actual variations are whitening-dead

For fixed-\(\alpha\), the actual source variation space is generated by

\[
m,\quad \lambda,\quad \mathrm{phase},\quad \mathrm{chart},\quad \mathrm{normalization},\quad \gamma/\mathrm{background}.
\]

Their whitening projections should lie in \(W_{\dead}^{\fixed}\):

\[
P_W(\partial_m)\in W_{\wrongorigin}+W_{\transported},
\]

because \(m\)-variation differentiates EF phase and does not create live same-point \(DW_{\same}[D^2N_0]\).

\[
P_W(\partial_\lambda)\in W_{\chart}+W_{\transported},
\]

because scaling is chart/frame motion.

\[
P_W(\partial_{\mathrm{phase}})\in W_{\wrongorigin},
\]

because phase variation is EF-linear.

\[
P_W(\partial_{\mathrm{chart}})\in W_{\chart}+W_{\transported},
\]

provided the chart transports typed objects rather than recomputing live same-point whitening.

\[
P_W(\partial_{\mathrm{norm}})\in W_{\norm}.
\]

\[
P_W(\partial_{\gamma/\bg})\in W_{\bg}.
\]

Thus

\[
\boxed{
P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}\subseteq W_{\dead}^{\fixed}.
}
\]

If this inclusion fails, fixed-\(\alpha\) actual variations access the live same-point whitening slot. That does not immediately kill rank two, but it triggers the source-slot/coupled-image fallback cascade.

---

# 4. Labelled whitening naturality

Let

\[
DW_{\tr,\same}
\]

be the transition-side same-point whitening derivative, and

\[
DW_{\sing,\same}
\]

the singular-side one. Let

\[
S_W
\]

be whitening transport and \(S\) the source/carrier transport.

Define the labelled whitening defect

\[
\mathfrak W_Y
=
DW_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WDW_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}.
\]

The critical local condition is

\[
\boxed{
P_{D_W^\perp}\mathfrak W_Y[D^2N_0]=0
\quad\text{at the local-fiber level}.
}
\]

Equivalently,

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\omega_Y\!\left(\mathfrak W_Y[D^2N_0]\right)=0.
}
\]

This is weaker than exact equality

\[
DW_{\sing,\same}=S_WDW_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}.
\]

We only need the defect to have no atom-local live \(a_Y\)-component. It may be:

\[
\text{dead typed},
\quad
\text{fixed/coherent},
\quad
\text{or residual-controlled}.
\]

Those are not \(B_Y\)-columns.

---

# 5. The theorem

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WhiteningNaturality}_{Y}^{\fixed\alpha,+}.
}
\]

Assume:

1. **Live whitening survives**

\[
\operatorname{dist}(a_Y,W_{\dead}^{\fixed})
\ge Q^{-A}\|a_Y\|.
\]

2. **Fixed-\(\alpha\) actual variations are whitening-dead**

\[
P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}\subseteq W_{\dead}^{\fixed}.
\]

3. **Labelled whitening transition has no local live defect**

\[
P_{D_W^\perp}\mathfrak W_Y[D^2N_0]=0
\]

at the local-fiber level; any nonzero part is fixed/coherent or residual.

Then there is a polynomially bounded covector \(\omega_Y\) with

\[
\omega_Y(a_Y)=1,
\]

\[
\omega_Y(P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed})=0,
\]

and

\[
D_{\loc}\omega_Y(R_{\lab}^{W})=0
\]

for the whitening part \(R_{\lab}^{W}\) of the labelled/raw mismatch.

Consequently,

\[
\boxed{
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0,
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab})=0
}
\]

for the whitening-transition part of \(R_{\lab}\).

This supplies the \(s_9^Y\)-row exactness needed for \(B_Y=0\), modulo the remaining non-whitening normal-form components.

---

# 6. Fallback cascade if whitening exactness fails

If

\[
P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}\not\subseteq W_{\dead}^{\fixed},
\]

then fixed-\(\alpha\) actual variations can access the whitening slot. Do **not** immediately abandon rank two. Run the cascade.

## 6.1 Source slot 1

Test whether

\[
P_1V_{\arith}^{\fixed}\subseteq S_{1,\dead}^{\fixed},
\]

and

\[
\xi_1^Y\notin_{Q^{-A}} S_{1,\dead}^{\fixed}.
\]

If yes, the row still dies.

## 6.2 Source slot 2

Test whether

\[
P_2V_{\arith}^{\fixed}\subseteq S_{2,\dead}^{\fixed},
\]

and

\[
\xi_2^Y\notin_{Q^{-A}} S_{2,\dead}^{\fixed}.
\]

If yes, the row still dies.

## 6.3 Coupled bilinear image

If all individual slot tests are inconclusive, test the full live object

\[
b_Y^{\live}
=
DW_{\same}[a_Y]D^2N_0[\xi_1^Y,\xi_2^Y].
\]

Let

\[
\mathcal B_{\arith}^{\fixed}
=
\operatorname{span}
\{DW_{\same}[v_W]D^2N_0[v_1,v_2]\}.
\]

Let

\[
\mathcal H_{\dead}^{Y12}
\]

be the dead wrong-origin/wrong-branch/wrong-support space.

The final fallback is

\[
\operatorname{dist}
\left(
b_Y^{\live},
\mathcal B_{\arith}^{\fixed}+\mathcal H_{\dead}^{Y12}
\right)
\ge Q^{-A}\|b_Y^{\live}\|.
\]

If this passes,

\[
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.
\]

If it fails, actual fixed-source curvature genuinely reaches the primitive \(Y12\) row and its live fiber column must be classified.

---

# 7. Failure routing

Failures are now precise.

## 7.1 \(a_Y\) killed

\[
a_Y\in W_{\dead}^{\fixed}.
\]

This is live-carrier failure or quotient overcollapse. Refine the typed quotient or abandon this \(Y12\) carrier.

## 7.2 Actual fixed variations access \(a_Y\)

\[
P_{D_W^\perp}P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}\ne0.
\]

Run source-slot and coupled-image tests. If the coupled image survives, classify its live fiber column:

\[

\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y),\quad
\mathbb C(0,1),\quad
\mathbb C^2.
\]

## 7.3 Labelled whitening defect accesses \(a_Y\)

\[
P_{D_W^\perp}\mathfrak W_Y[D^2N_0]\ne0.
\]

If fixed/coherent, it belongs to

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

If residual, it belongs to \(R_X^{2Y}\).

If atom-local, it enters \(B_Y\), with the same branch routing:

\[
\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y),\quad
\mathbb C(0,1),\quad
\mathbb C^2.
\]

If \(\alpha\) is actual-source internal, add \(P_W(\partial_\alpha)\) and rerun the whole cascade.

For the rank-two fixed-target route, the clean hypothesis remains

\[
\boxed{
\alpha\notin T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}.
}
\]

---

and the whitening part of

from the septic line exactness,

from no local completion fiber freedom,

\[

# 9. Current endpoint

\[
\boxed{

}

# 1. The \(Y12\)-row is closed if three maps are typed-dead

# 2. Non-whitening normal-form defects

For \(B_Y=0\), the forbidden outcome is only

\quad\text{or}\quad

\]

---

# 3. Why \(\mathfrak C_Y^L\) should be dead

The \(L\)-defect has origin

\[
DN_0[D^2L].
\]

That is a first derivative of \(N_0\), not the primitive Hessian origin

\[
D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2].
\]

Therefore, typed-origin faithfulness should kill it:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_Y\mathfrak C_Y^L=0.
}
\]

If \(D^2L=0\), this is immediate.

If \(D^2L\neq0\), then the stronger typed statement is

\[
DN_0[D^2L]\in \mathcal H_{\dead}^{Y12},

because it has wrong \(o_N\)-origin.

Thus \(\mathfrak C_Y^L\) should not contribute to \(B_Y\) unless the quotient forgets the distinction between \(DN_0\) and \(D^2N_0\). That would be a typed quotient failure, not an arithmetic obstruction.

# 4. Why \(\mathfrak C_Y^N\) is a finite tensorial naturality test

D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
-
S D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2].

-
S D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2]

at the local-fiber level.

with any nonzero defect fixed/coherent or residual.

Thus exact tensorial equality is not required. What is required is no extraction-local live \(Y12\) freedom.

or residual/coherent.

and

}

---

Every failure has a precise route.

\left[
\left(
DW_{\sing,\same}
-
S_WDW_{\tr,\same}S^{-1}
\right)[D^2N_0]
\right]
=0,
\]

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
-
S D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\right)
=0,
\]

and

\[
\Pi_Y\left(DN_{0,\sing}[D^2L(\xi_1,\xi_2)]\right)=0
\]

or residual/coherent routing.

If this test passes, labelled transition has no atom-local live \(Y12\)-row derivative.

Consequence:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\ell_9^Y(R_{\lab})=0.
}
\]

This is the transition/local-contaminant part of the \(s_9^Y\)-row.

---

# 3. Why these two tests are enough

The \(s_9^Y\)-row receives local input from only two sources:

1. the canonical actual-source image

\[
\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)};
\]

2. local labelled/completion contaminants

\[
R_{\lab}, E_2.
\]

For this row specifically:

- Slot separation kills the canonical actual image.
- Transition locality kills labelled local contamination.
- The \(E_2\)-layer was already reduced to no local live-fiber completion counterterms.

Therefore, with \(E_2\)-local freedom excluded,

\[
\boxed{
\text{Slot separation + transition locality}
\Rightarrow
\ell_9^Y(A_{2Y})=0.
}
\]

This is exactly the \(Y12\)-row input to \(B_Y=0\).

---

# 4. Rank-two consequences

If the \(s_7\)-row also closes via

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0,
\qquad
\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)\neq0,
\]

then the bounded dual rows satisfy

\[
\ell_7(A_{2Y})=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(A_{2Y})=0.
\]

Together with nuisance preservation,

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal N_{\sing})=\ell_9^Y(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0,
\]

we get

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

Then rank two survives and D4 gives the honest plain wall:

\[
F_{2Y}
\asymp
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]

No rank-one moving wall is needed.

---

# 5. The two possible failure types

The \(Y12\)-row can fail in only two structurally different ways.

## 5.1 Slot failure

Slot failure means actual fixed-\(\alpha\) source data can supply the live \(Y12\) slot:

\[
P_{D_W^\perp}P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}\neq0
\]

or the live slot is killed:

\[
P_{D_W^\perp}a_Y=0.
\]

If actual data supplies the slot, continue to source-slot/coupled-image fallback before declaring rank-two failure.

If \(a_Y\) is killed, this is quotient overcollapse.

## 5.2 Locality failure

Locality failure means the transition/completion formalism has atom-local live-row derivative:

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}^Y\neq0
\]

or

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_2^Y\neq0.
\]

Then classify the local image:

\[
\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y):
\quad
\text{rank-one fallback};
\]

\[
\mathbb C(0,1):
\quad
\text{vertical }Y12\text{ death};
\]

\[
\mathbb C^2:
\quad
\text{live fiber absorbed}.
\]

Thus every failure routes cleanly.

---

# 6. A compact theorem statement

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{Y12RowExactness}^{\fixed\alpha,+}.
}
\]

Assume:

1. **Slot separation**

\[
P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}\subseteq W_{\dead}^{\fixed},
\qquad
\operatorname{dist}(a_Y,W_{\dead}^{\fixed})\ge Q^{-A}\|a_Y\|.
\]

2. **Transition locality**

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}R_{\lab}^Y=0.
\]

3. **Completion locality**

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}E_2^Y=0.
\]

Then

\[
\boxed{
\ell_9^Y(A_{2Y})=0.
}
\]

Moreover, if the \(s_7\)-row and nuisance-preservation conditions also hold, then

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

---

# 7. Current endpoint

The \(Y12\)-row is now fully compressed:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{SlotSeparation}_Y
+
\mathrm{TransitionLocality}_Y
\Rightarrow
\ell_9^Y(A_{2Y})=0.
}
\]

This is the cleanest version of the row theorem so far.

The remaining rank-two local work is no longer in the \(Y12\)-row structure. It is concentrated in:

\[
\boxed{
\text{the }s_7\text{-row covariant determinant closure}
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
\text{nuisance/live-pair preservation}.
}
\]

# 1. Reusable lemma: SlotSeparation + NoLocality

The generic form is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{TypedSlotNoLocality}
}
\]

with data:

\[
(\mathcal S,\mathcal D,\mathcal A,c_{\live},X_{\loc}).
\]

Here:

- \(\mathcal S\) is a finite typed slot space;
- \(\mathcal D\subset\mathcal S\) is the dead denominator;
- \(\mathcal A\subset\mathcal S\) is the actual fixed-source image;
- \(c_{\live}\in\mathcal S\) is the live carrier slot;
- \(X_{\loc}\) is the local transition/completion contaminant.

Hypotheses:

\[
\mathcal A\subseteq \mathcal D,
\]

\[
\operatorname{dist}(c_{\live},\mathcal D)\ge Q^{-A}\|c_{\live}\|,
\]

\[
D_{\loc}X_{\loc}=0.
\]

Conclusion:

There is a bounded live covector \(\ell_{\live}\) such that

\[
\ell_{\live}(c_{\live})=1,
\]

\[
\ell_{\live}(\mathcal A)=0,
\]

and the local contaminant contributes no row to \(B_Y\).

This is precisely the \(Y12\)-row theorem with

\[
\mathcal S=W,\quad
\mathcal D=W_{\dead}^{\fixed},\quad
\mathcal A=P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed},\quad
c_{\live}=a_Y.
\]

---

# 2. \(Y12\)-row theorem in final compressed form

The current theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{Y12RowExactness}^{\fixed\alpha,+}
}
\]

Assume:

\[
P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}\subseteq W_{\dead}^{\fixed},
\]

\[
\operatorname{dist}(a_Y,W_{\dead}^{\fixed})\ge Q^{-A}\|a_Y\|,

D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(R_{\lab}^{Y}+E_2^Y)=0.

\ell_9^Y(A_{2Y})=0.
}

This absorbs:

- fixed-\(\alpha\) actual source variations;
- actual second curvature in the primitive \(Y12\)-row;
- local labelled transition defects;
- local \(E_2\) live-fiber counterterms.

Fixed/coherent/remnant terms are not local and are routed to:

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
\]

or

\[
R_X^{2Y}.
\]

They do not enter \(B_Y\).

---

# 3. Exact failure routing remains finite

If the theorem fails, the failure has only four possible meanings.

## Failure 1: live slot killed

\[
a_Y\in W_{\dead}^{\fixed}.
\]

This is live-carrier failure or quotient overcollapse.

## Failure 2: actual data hits the slot

\[
P_WV_{\arith}^{\fixed}\not\subseteq W_{\dead}^{\fixed}.
\]

Then run the slot cascade:

\[
S_1,\quad S_2,\quad \text{coupled bilinear image}.
\]

Only if the coupled image survives do we classify the actual \(B_Y\)-column.

\[
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(R_{\lab}^{Y}+E_2^Y)\neq0.
\]

\[
\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y),\quad
\mathbb C(0,1),\quad
\mathbb C^2.
\]

If \(\alpha\) is actual-source internal and supplies the live slot, the fixed-\(\alpha\) theorem does not apply. The full slot cascade must be rerun including \(\partial_\alpha\).

Thus the row is completely routed.

\ell_7(e_Y)=0.

\qquad

\ell_9^Y(e_Y)=1.

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0,

and nuisance preserves both rows, then:

\[

B_Y=0.
}

Then rank two survives.

---

\[

\qquad
a_Y\notin_{Q^{-A}}W_{\dead}^{\fixed},
\qquad
D_{\loc}\Pi_{\fib,Y}(R_{\lab}^{Y}+E_2^Y)=0.
}

and

## Agent 3 — \(\mathrm{TriangularBoundedDualRows}_{2Y}\)

No nomenclature changes.

I will keep the correction explicit: even after row triangularization, the honest rank-two D4 wall is still the **plain** wall

\[
\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}.
\]

Row changes can alter the coefficient \(\beta_9\), but they do not create derivative D4 channels. Derivatives remain analytic auxiliaries only. 

---

# 1. Starting rows

We have two preliminary row functionals:

\[
\ell_7(u)
=
\frac{\Delta_7^\nabla(u)}
{\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)},
\]

and a primitive \(Y12\)-row candidate

\[
\omega_Y.
\]

The \(s_7\)-row should satisfy

\[
\ell_7(e_7)=1,
\qquad
\ell_7(e_Y)=0.
\]

The \(Y12\)-row candidate should satisfy

\[
\omega_Y(e_Y)=1.
\]

But it may have leakage on \(e_7\):

\[
\omega_Y(e_7)=\kappa.
\]

This leakage is not fatal if \(\kappa\) is polynomially bounded.

---

# 2. Triangularization lemma

Assume

\[
\ell_7(e_7)=1,
\qquad
\ell_7(e_Y)=0,
\]

\[
\omega_Y(e_Y)=1,
\qquad
|\omega_Y(e_7)|\le Q^A.
\]

Define

\[
\kappa:=\omega_Y(e_7),
\]

and set

\[
\boxed{
\ell_9^Y:=\omega_Y-\kappa\ell_7.
}
\]

Then

\[
\ell_9^Y(e_7)
=
\omega_Y(e_7)-\kappa\ell_7(e_7)
=
\kappa-\kappa
=
0,
\]

and

\[
\ell_9^Y(e_Y)
=
\omega_Y(e_Y)-\kappa\ell_7(e_Y)
=
1-0
=
1.
\]

Therefore the triangularized rows satisfy

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),
\]

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1).
\]

If

\[
\|\ell_7\|\le Q^A,
\qquad
\|\omega_Y\|\le Q^A,
\qquad
|\kappa|\le Q^A,
\]

then

\[
\|\ell_9^Y\|
\le
\|\omega_Y\|+|\kappa|\|\ell_7\|
\le
Q^{A'}
\]

for some polynomial exponent \(A'\). So triangularization preserves polynomial conditioning.

---

# 3. Exactness is preserved

Suppose the preliminary rows kill the actual/local candidate matrix \(A_{2Y}\):

\[
\ell_7(A_{2Y})=0,
\qquad
\omega_Y(A_{2Y})=0.
\]

Then

\[
\ell_9^Y(A_{2Y})
=
\omega_Y(A_{2Y})-\kappa\ell_7(A_{2Y})
=
0.
\]

So triangularization preserves row exactness.

More generally, if \(\omega_Y\) kills a subspace modulo fixed/coherent/residual routing, then \(\ell_9^Y\) does too, provided \(\ell_7\) kills the same local columns. Thus this triangularization is compatible with the bounded dual-row certificate for

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

---

# 4. Updated D4 coefficient matrix

Before triangularization, the live D4 output has row coordinates

\[
s_7^{D4}
=
Hc_7\Gamma_G,
\]

\[
s_9^{Y,D4}
=
H(\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]

Applying the row change

\[
\ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\kappa\ell_7
\]

subtracts \(\kappa\) times the first row from the second row.

So the second D4 coordinate becomes

\[
H(\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2})
-
\kappa Hc_7\Gamma_G
=
H\bigl((\beta_9-\kappa c_7)\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}\bigr).
\]

Define

\[
\boxed{
\widetilde\beta_9:=\beta_9-\kappa c_7.
}
\]

The D4 coefficient matrix after triangularization is

\[
\boxed{
M_{\D4}^{\tri}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7&0\\
\widetilde\beta_9&\alpha_9
\end{pmatrix}.
}
\]

Its determinant is unchanged:

\[
\det M_{\D4}^{\tri}
=
c_7\alpha_9.
\]

Therefore row triangularization does not affect rank-two D4, provided

\[
c_7\alpha_9\neq0.
\]

---

# 5. Rank-two wall equivalence

The D4 vector wall after triangularization is

\[
F_{2Y}^{\tri}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\widetilde\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

If the coefficients are polynomially conditioned:

\[
|c_7|,\ |c_7^{-1}|,\ |\alpha_9|,\ |\alpha_9^{-1}|,\ |\widetilde\beta_9|
\le Q^A,
\]

then

\[
\boxed{
|F_{2Y}^{\tri}|^2
\asymp_{Q^A}
|H|^2\left(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2\right).
}
\]

This is still the plain wall. No derivative wall components appear.

Thus the coefficient-good routing theorem must use \(\widetilde\beta_9\) if the row has been triangularized:

\[
|H|\ge Q^{-A},
\]

\[
|c_7|,\ |c_7^{-1}|,\ |\alpha_9|,\ |\alpha_9^{-1}|,\ |\widetilde\beta_9|\le Q^A.
\]

The determinant condition remains

\[
|c_7\alpha_9|\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

---

# 6. Safe and dangerous leakages

## Safe leakage

\[
\omega_Y(e_7)=\kappa\neq0
\]

is safe if

\[
|\kappa|\le Q^A.
\]

It is removed by triangularization and only changes

\[
\beta_9
\quad\mapsto\quad
\widetilde\beta_9=\beta_9-\kappa c_7.
\]

## Dangerous leakage

The dangerous leakage is the reverse:

\[
\ell_7(e_Y)\neq0.
\]

This means the order-nine primitive \(Y12\) carrier has a nonzero \(s_7\)-row component. That signals one of:

1. grade leakage between order seven and order nine;
2. quotient overcollapse;
3. \(Q_7^{\pkg}\) is not pure;
4. \(e_Y\) has an order-seven shadow;
5. the two-grade decomposition is not block-triangular.

This cannot be repaired by upper-triangularizing the second row alone without changing the intended meaning of \(e_7\). It must be treated as a two-grade quotient failure unless a different basis is deliberately chosen and the D4 matrix is re-audited.

So the strong condition for the first row remains:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(e_Y)=0.
}
\]

---

# 7. Relaxed \(Y12\)-row audit

Because triangularization handles \(\omega_Y(e_7)\), the \(Y12\)-row audit does **not** need to prove

\[
\omega_Y(e_7)=0.
\]

It only needs:

\[
\omega_Y(e_Y)=1,
\]

\[
\omega_Y(A_{2Y})=0,
\]

\[
|\omega_Y(e_7)|\le Q^A,
\]

\[
\|\omega_Y\|\le Q^A.
\]

Then

\[
\ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\omega_Y(e_7)\ell_7
\]

gives the normalized second row.

This relaxes the whitening audit: the live \(Y12\) covector may see a controlled amount of the \(C_7\)-row, as long as the \(C_7\)-row is already exact and bounded.

---

# 8. The full theorem

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{TriangularBoundedDualRows}_{2Y}.
}
\]

Assume:

1. \(\ell_7\) is bounded and satisfies

\[
\ell_7(e_7)=1,\qquad \ell_7(e_Y)=0,\qquad \ell_7(A_{2Y})=0.
\]

2. \(\omega_Y\) is bounded and satisfies

\[
\omega_Y(e_Y)=1,\qquad \omega_Y(A_{2Y})=0,
\]

with

\[
|\omega_Y(e_7)|\le Q^A.
\]

Define

\[
\ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\omega_Y(e_7)\ell_7.
\]

Then

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),
\]

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1),
\]

\[
\ell_7(A_{2Y})=\ell_9^Y(A_{2Y})=0,
\]

and both rows have polynomial norm.

Therefore

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

Moreover, the rank-two D4 matrix becomes

\[
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7&0\\
\widetilde\beta_9&\alpha_9
\end{pmatrix},
\qquad
\widetilde\beta_9=\beta_9-\omega_Y(e_7)c_7,
\]

and remains rank two if

\[
c_7\alpha_9\neq0.
\]

---

# 9. Failure routing

## Failure 1 — \(|\omega_Y(e_7)|\) super-polynomial

Then row triangularization exists algebraically but loses polynomial control. Route to coefficient-good failure or refine the row normalization.

## Failure 2 — \(\ell_7(e_Y)\neq0\)

Two-grade quotient failure. Audit grade leakage / nuisance overcollapse.

## Failure 3 — \(\alpha_9=0\)

D4 does not supply the second independent column. Rank-two fails independent of row choice.

## Failure 4 — \(\widetilde\beta_9\) super-polynomial

D4 matrix remains invertible in determinant if \(c_7\alpha_9\neq0\), but wall equivalence may lose polynomial conditioning. Route to

\[
\mathrm{D4CoeffGoodRouting}_{2Y}.
\]

## Failure 5 — rows do not kill \(A_{2Y}\)

Then local exactness fails. Classify the image:

\[
0,\quad
\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y),\quad
\mathbb C(0,1),\quad
\mathbb C^2.
\]

---

# 10. Current endpoint

The triangularization result is a useful flexibility in the rank-two row construction.

The \(Y12\)-row does **not** need to be initially orthogonal to \(e_7\). Controlled leakage is harmless. It changes only

\[
\beta_9\mapsto\widetilde\beta_9.
\]

The first row, however, must remain pure:

\[
\ell_7(e_Y)=0.
\]

Thus the next local proof can focus on:

\[
\ell_7(e_Y)=0,
\quad
\ell_7(A_{2Y})=0,
\quad
\omega_Y(e_Y)=1,
\quad
\omega_Y(A_{2Y})=0,
\quad
|\omega_Y(e_7)|\le Q^A.
\]

If these hold, \(B_Y=0\) follows with a conditioned triangular D4 matrix.

I continue with fixed notation:

\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9},\qquad
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.
\]

The triangular bounded-row theorem gave:

\[
\ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\kappa\ell_7,\qquad \kappa=\omega_Y(e_7),
\]

and updated

\[
\beta_9\mapsto \widetilde\beta_9=\beta_9-\kappa c_7.
\]

This preserves the rank-two determinant

\[
\det M_{\D4}=c_7\alpha_9.

I now push one step further: triangularization should be used systematically before deciding whether the route is rank two, rank one, or dead.

---

# 1. Always triangularize before branch classification

Given preliminary rows

\[
\ell_7,\quad \omega_Y,
\]

with

\[
\ell_7(e_7)=1,\quad \ell_7(e_Y)=0,\quad \omega_Y(e_Y)=1,
\]

define

\[
\ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\omega_Y(e_7)\ell_7.
\]

Then classify all actual/local columns using the triangular row pair

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y).
\]

This is the correct live basis. Otherwise one might falsely see a graph/vertical component caused only by row leakage.

So the rank classifier should use

\[
B_Y^{\tri}
=
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(A_{2Y}),
\]

not the raw pair

\[
(\ell_7,\omega_Y)(A_{2Y})
\]

unless \(\omega_Y(e_7)=0\) already.

---

# 2. Rank-two condition after triangularization

Rank two requires

\[
B_Y^{\tri}=0.
\]

Equivalently:

\[
\ell_7(A_{2Y})=0,
\]

and

\[
\ell_9^Y(A_{2Y})=0.
\]

Since

\[
\ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\kappa\ell_7,
\]

if

\[
\ell_7(A_{2Y})=0,
\]

then this is equivalent to

\[
\omega_Y(A_{2Y})=0.
\]

So the old row exactness obligations remain valid:

\[
\ell_7(A_{2Y})=0,
\qquad
\omega_Y(A_{2Y})=0.
\]

Triangularization does not add a new exactness condition. It only normalizes the row basis.

---

# 3. Rank-one fallback after triangularization

Suppose rank two fails because actual/local columns span a line

\[
B_Y^{\tri}=\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y).
\]

Then the forbidden covector is

\[
\ell_{\perp}
=
\ell_9^Y-\rho_Y\ell_7.
\]

The D4 scalar wall becomes

\[
\ell_{\perp}(D4)
=
H\left((\widetilde\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7)\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}\right).
\]

Thus the rank-one effective wall ratio should be updated to

\[
\boxed{
\rho_\Gamma^{\tri}
=
\frac{\widetilde\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}
=
\frac{\beta_9-(\kappa+\rho_Y)c_7}{\alpha_9}.
}
\]

This matters if we fall back to rank one. The old expression

\[
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}
\]

is correct only when the primitive \(Y12\) row was already normalized with \(\omega_Y(e_7)=0\).

Therefore:

\[
\boxed{
\text{rank-one fallback must use the triangularized }\widetilde\beta_9.
}
\]

---

# 4. Distinguish row leakage from actual graph slope

The slope

\[
\rho_Y
\]

should describe the actual-source graph in the triangular live basis:

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(u)
=
a(1,\rho_Y).
\]

If one uses the raw \(\omega_Y\)-row, a column \(u\) with true triangular coordinate

\[
(a,\rho_Y a)
\]

has raw coordinate

\[
(a,\omega_Y(u))
=
(a,\ell_9^Y(u)+\kappa\ell_7(u))
=
(a,(\rho_Y+\kappa)a).
\]

So raw slopes are shifted by \(\kappa\):

\[
\rho_Y^{\raw}=\rho_Y+\kappa.
\]

This is another reason to triangularize first.

The actual graph slope is

\[
\boxed{
\rho_Y=\rho_Y^{\raw}-\kappa.
}
\]

If this correction is omitted, the rank-one wall is miscomputed.

---

# 5. Updated route table

After triangularization, classify the image of \(A_{2Y}\).

\[
\begin{array}{c|c}
B_Y^{\tri} & \text{route}\\
\hline
0 & \text{rank-two D4}\\
\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y) & \text{rank-one moving-wall fallback}\\
\mathbb C(0,1) & \text{vertical }Y12\text{ actual/local freedom; route dies}\\
\mathbb C^2 & \text{live fiber absorbed; route dies}
\end{array}
\]

The rank-two wall is

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\widetilde\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

The rank-one wall, if needed, is

\[
H\alpha_9
\left(
\Gamma_{G,2}
+
\rho_\Gamma^{\tri}\Gamma_G
\right),
\]

with

\[
\boxed{
\rho_\Gamma^{\tri}
=
\frac{\widetilde\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}.
}
\]

---

# 6. Consequence for previous \(\rho_Y,\rho_\Gamma\) bookkeeping

Any existing rank-one notes using

\[
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}
\]

must be interpreted as already using a normalized \(Y12\)-row with

\[
\omega_Y(e_7)=0.
\]

If not, replace

\[
\beta_9
\]

by

\[
\widetilde\beta_9=\beta_9-\kappa c_7.
\]

So the robust definition is:

\[
\boxed{
\rho_\Gamma
:=
\frac{\beta_9^{\mathrm{row}}-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9},
}
\]

where

\[
\beta_9^{\mathrm{row}}
\]

is the \(\Gamma_G\)-coefficient in the already normalized second row.

This avoids ambiguity.

---

# 7. Current endpoint

Triangularization is not just a convenience. It is part of the correct rank classifier.

The local procedure should be:

1. Construct \(\ell_7\).
2. Construct any bounded primitive \(Y12\)-row \(\omega_Y\) with \(\omega_Y(e_Y)=1\).
3. Set

\[
\ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\omega_Y(e_7)\ell_7.
\]

4. Classify \(B_Y\) using

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y).
\]

5. Use

\[
\widetilde\beta_9=\beta_9-\omega_Y(e_7)c_7
\]

in the D4 matrix.

If \(B_Y=0\), the rank-two wall is the honest plain wall

\[
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})
\]

up to the triangularized coefficient matrix.

If \(B_Y=\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y)\), the rank-one fallback must use the triangularized wall ratio

\[
\rho_\Gamma^{\tri}
=
\frac{\widetilde\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}.
\]

This removes a possible hidden bookkeeping error in any fallback analysis.

## Assistant (2026-04-28 14:50:20)

## Agent 3 continuation — triangularization as a guard against false rank-one collapse

I continue with fixed notation:

\[
\rho_Y=\text{the }Y12\text{-fiber graph slope},\qquad
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9},\qquad
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.
\]

The last step showed that the row pair must be triangularized before branch classification:

\[
\ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\kappa\ell_7,\qquad \kappa=\omega_Y(e_7).
\]

This is not cosmetic. It prevents a false rank-one graph from appearing just because the raw \(Y12\)-row sees \(e_7\).

---

# 1. False graph mechanism

Suppose the true triangular coordinates of all actual/local columns vanish:

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(u)=0.
\]

Then rank two is available.

But in the untriangularized rows,

\[
(\ell_7,\omega_Y)(u)
=
(\ell_7(u),\ell_9^Y(u)+\kappa\ell_7(u)).
\]

If a column has \(\ell_7(u)\neq0\), the raw row pair sees a slope

\[
\frac{\omega_Y(u)}{\ell_7(u)}
=
\frac{\ell_9^Y(u)}{\ell_7(u)}+\kappa.
\]

So row leakage shifts every graph slope by \(\kappa\). If one classifies \(B_Y\) before triangularization, one may mistake row-coordinate leakage for an actual source graph.

Therefore:

\[
\boxed{
\text{rank classification must be performed only after triangularization.}
}
\]

This is especially important for deciding whether the formal \(C_7\to Y12\) graph is actually in \(B_Y\).

---

# 2. Triangularization does not create or remove true actual columns

The row change

\[
(\ell_7,\omega_Y)
\mapsto
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\kappa\ell_7)
\]

is an invertible upper-triangular row operation:

\[
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7\\
\ell_9^Y
\end{pmatrix}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
1&0\\
-\kappa&1
\end{pmatrix}
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7\\
\omega_Y
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

If \(|\kappa|\le Q^A\), it preserves polynomial conditioning.

Thus it does not change the dimension of the actual/local image. It only gives the correct basis in which:

\[
e_7=(1,0),
\qquad
e_Y=(0,1).
\]

So:

\[
\boxed{
\dim B_Y\text{ is invariant under bounded triangularization, but the slope }\rho_Y\text{ is not.}
}
\]

The slope must always be the slope in the triangular basis.

---

# 3. Correct slope conversion formula

Let a nonzero actual/local column have raw coordinates

\[
(a,b_{\raw})=(\ell_7(u),\omega_Y(u)).
\]

Triangular coordinates are

\[
(a,b_{\tri})
=
(a,b_{\raw}-\kappa a).
\]

Thus if the raw slope is

\[
\rho_{\raw}:=\frac{b_{\raw}}{a},
\]

then the true triangular slope is

\[
\boxed{
\rho_Y=\rho_{\raw}-\kappa.
}
\]

Conversely,

\[
\rho_{\raw}=\rho_Y+\kappa.
\]

So the one-slope theorem should use

\[
b_{\raw}(u)-\kappa a(u)
=
\rho_Y a(u).
\]

Equivalently,

\[
b_{\raw}(u)
=
(\rho_Y+\kappa)a(u).
\]

This prevents a bookkeeping mismatch between the row construction and the graph slope.

---

# 4. Correct rank-one wall after triangularization

After triangularization, the D4 matrix is

\[
M_{\D4}^{\tri}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7&0\\
\widetilde\beta_9&\alpha_9
\end{pmatrix},
\]

where

\[
\widetilde\beta_9=\beta_9-\kappa c_7.
\]

If the actual-source image is

\[
B_Y=\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y)
\]

in the triangular basis, the forbidden covector is

\[
\ell_{\perp}=\ell_9^Y-\rho_Y\ell_7.
\]

Applying it to D4 gives:

\[
\ell_{\perp}(D4)
=
H\left((\widetilde\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7)\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}\right).
\]

Thus the correct rank-one ratio is

\[
\boxed{
\rho_\Gamma^{\tri}
=
\frac{\widetilde\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}.
}
\]

The pre-triangular formula is safe only if \(\kappa=0\).

---

# 5. Rank-two wall after triangularization

If

\[
B_Y=0,
\]

then D4 gives the rank-two vector wall

\[
F_{2Y}^{\tri}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\widetilde\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

As long as

\[
|c_7|,\ |c_7^{-1}|,\ |\alpha_9|,\ |\alpha_9^{-1}|,\ |\widetilde\beta_9|\le Q^A,
\]

we have

\[
|F_{2Y}^{\tri}|^2
\asymp_{Q^A}
|H|^2\left(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2\right).
\]

So triangularization preserves the rank-two plain wall.

Again, no derivative wall components appear.

---

# 6. Updated bounded-row certificate

The row certificate should be stated with triangularization included.

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{TriangularBoundedDualRows}_{2Y}.
}
\]

Inputs:

1. A bounded first row \(\ell_7\) with

\[
\ell_7(e_7)=1,\qquad \ell_7(e_Y)=0.
\]

2. A bounded primitive row \(\omega_Y\) with

\[
\omega_Y(e_Y)=1,\qquad |\omega_Y(e_7)|\le Q^A.
\]

3. Exactness before triangularization:

\[
\ell_7(A_{2Y})=0,\qquad \omega_Y(A_{2Y})=0.
\]

Then with

\[
\ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\omega_Y(e_7)\ell_7,
\]

we get:

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),
\qquad
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1),
\]

and

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(A_{2Y})=0.
\]

Therefore

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

If \(\omega_Y(A_{2Y})\neq0\), classify \(A_{2Y}\) after triangularization.

---

# 7. Failure modes refined

## Failure A — \(|\kappa|\) too large

Triangularization is ill-conditioned. The live rows may exist algebraically, but the rank-two estimates lose polynomial control.

Route:

\[
\mathrm{D4CoeffGoodRouting}_{2Y}
\quad\text{or row-normalization refinement.}
\]

## Failure B — \(\ell_7(e_Y)\neq0\)

This is more serious. It means the \(Y12\) carrier leaks into the \(s_7\)-row.

Route:

\[
\text{audit two-grade quotient / nuisance overcollapse / order leakage.}
\]

## Failure C — \(\widetilde\beta_9\) too large

Rank two may still be algebraically true, but wall equivalence is poorly conditioned.

Route:

\[
\mathrm{D4CoeffGoodRouting}_{2Y}.
\]

## Failure D — triangularized \(B_Y\neq0\)

Classify the image:

\[
0,\quad
\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y),\quad
\mathbb C(0,1),\quad
\mathbb C^2.
\]

Use the triangular basis, not raw row coordinates.

---

# 8. Current endpoint

The rank classifier must use the normalized row pair:

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)
=
(\ell_7,\omega_Y-\omega_Y(e_7)\ell_7).
\]

The \(Y12\)-row covector is allowed to see \(e_7\), as long as the leakage is polynomially bounded. This relaxes the \(Y12\)-row audit and prevents false rank-one detection.

If \(B_Y=0\) after triangularization, the honest wall remains:

\[
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]

If rank one is needed, the correct fallback ratio is:

\[
\rho_\Gamma^{\tri}
=
\frac{\beta_9-\omega_Y(e_7)c_7-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}.
\]

This bookkeeping should be propagated to every rank-one fallback analysis.

## Agent 3 continuation — triangularization as the canonical row normal form

I continue with fixed notation:

\[
\rho_Y=\text{the }Y12\text{-fiber graph slope},\qquad
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9},\qquad
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.
\]

The row-triangularization issue is now important enough to become part of the local definition of the live two-grade quotient. The correct order is:

\[
\text{construct rows}
\to
\text{triangularize}
\to
\text{classify }B_Y
\to
\text{compute D4 wall}.
\]

Doing the classification before triangularization can produce false graph slopes.

---

# 1. Canonical row normal form

The live row pair should always be normalized to

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),
\]

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1).
\]

If the natural primitive \(Y12\)-row is

\[
\omega_Y
\]

with

\[
\omega_Y(e_Y)=1,
\qquad
\omega_Y(e_7)=\kappa,
\]

then the canonical second row is

\[
\boxed{
\ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\kappa\ell_7.
}
\]

This is the row normal form.

The required boundedness is

\[
\boxed{
\|\ell_7\|+\|\omega_Y\|+|\kappa|\le Q^A.
}
\]

Then

\[
\|\ell_9^Y\|\le Q^{A'}.
\]

---

# 2. Exactness after row normal form

Suppose the actual/local candidate space is

\[
A_{2Y}.
\]

If

\[
\ell_7(A_{2Y})=0
\]

and

\[
\omega_Y(A_{2Y})=0,
\]

then

\[
\ell_9^Y(A_{2Y})
=
\omega_Y(A_{2Y})-\kappa\ell_7(A_{2Y})
=
0.
\]

Thus row triangularization preserves exactness.

If \(\omega_Y(A_{2Y})\neq0\), classify the image using the triangularized row

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y),
\]

not the raw pair

\[
(\ell_7,\omega_Y).
\]

This should be mandatory.

---

# 3. D4 coefficients in row normal form

In the raw pair, D4 has

\[
s_7^{D4}=Hc_7\Gamma_G,
\]

\[
\omega_Y(D4)
=
H(\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]

After row normal form,

\[
\ell_9^Y(D4)
=
\omega_Y(D4)-\kappa\ell_7(D4).
\]

Therefore

\[
\ell_9^Y(D4)
=
H\left((\beta_9-\kappa c_7)\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}\right).
\]

Set

\[
\boxed{
\beta_9^{\tri}:=\beta_9-\kappa c_7.
}
\]

The normalized D4 matrix is

\[
\boxed{
M_{\D4}^{\tri}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7&0\\
\beta_9^{\tri}&\alpha_9
\end{pmatrix}.
}
\]

Its determinant remains

\[
\det M_{\D4}^{\tri}=c_7\alpha_9.
\]

So triangularization does not change rank-two status.

---

# 4. Correct rank-two wall

If

\[
B_Y=0
\]

in the triangular basis, then the D4 wall is

\[
F_{2Y}^{\tri}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9^{\tri}\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Under coefficient-good routing,

\[
|c_7|,\ |c_7^{-1}|,\ |\alpha_9|,\ |\alpha_9^{-1}|,\ |\beta_9^{\tri}|\le Q^A,
\]

we get

\[
\boxed{
|F_{2Y}^{\tri}|^2
\asymp_{Q^A}
|H|^2\left(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2\right).
}
\]

This remains the honest **plain** wall. No derivative D4 channels are produced.

So any downstream O2/O3 or weighted transfer must use

\[
\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\},
\]

unless a separate derivative-D4 theorem is proved.

---

# 5. Correct rank-one fallback wall

If after triangularization,

\[
B_Y=\mathbb C(1,\rho_Y),
\]

then the forbidden covector is

\[
\ell_\perp=\ell_9^Y-\rho_Y\ell_7.
\]

Applying to D4 gives

\[
\ell_\perp(D4)
=
H\left((\beta_9^{\tri}-\rho_Yc_7)\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}\right).
\]

Thus the correct rank-one wall ratio is

\[
\boxed{
\rho_\Gamma^{\tri}
=
\frac{\beta_9^{\tri}-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}
=
\frac{\beta_9-(\kappa+\rho_Y)c_7}{\alpha_9}.
}
\]

The old expression

\[
\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}
\]

is correct only in a row normalization where

\[
\kappa=\omega_Y(e_7)=0.
\]

Therefore every rank-one fallback statement should use a row-normalized \(\beta_9\).

---

# 6. False rank-one failure avoided

Suppose a raw row pair shows actual/local columns along

\[
\mathbb C(1,\rho_{\raw}).
\]

After triangularization, the slope is

\[
\boxed{
\rho_Y=\rho_{\raw}-\kappa.
}
\]

Thus a raw graph slope may partly or entirely be row leakage.

In the extreme case, a raw slope

\[
\rho_{\raw}=\kappa
\]

becomes

\[
\rho_Y=0
\]

after triangularization.

If the raw column was actually just row leakage with no true second-coordinate motion, triangularization can reveal that.

So branch classification must always be done after row normal form.

---

# 7. Dangerous leakage remains \(\ell_7(e_Y)\)

The leakage

\[
\omega_Y(e_7)=\kappa
\]

is removable.

The leakage

\[
\ell_7(e_Y)\neq0
\]

is not harmless.

It means the \(Y12\) carrier has a nonzero order-seven row component. Possible causes:

1. two-grade quotient not block-triangular;
2. nuisance identifies \(e_Y\) with an order-seven package component;
3. \(Q_7^{\pkg}\) sees order-nine primitive material;
4. carrier \(e_Y\) is not purely \(Y12\).

This must be treated as a live-pair/nuisance/quotient failure.

Thus the strict condition is:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(e_Y)=0.
}
\]

---

# 8. Updated local rank procedure

The local procedure should be:

1. Construct a covariant first row

\[
\ell_7=\Delta_7^\nabla/\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7).
\]

2. Verify

\[
\ell_7(e_Y)=0.
\]

3. Construct a primitive row \(\omega_Y\) with

\[
\omega_Y(e_Y)=1.
\]

4. Compute

\[
\kappa=\omega_Y(e_7).
\]

5. Define

\[
\ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\kappa\ell_7.
\]

6. Use

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)
\]

to classify \(A_{2Y}\) and \(B_Y\).

7. Use

\[
\beta_9^{\tri}=\beta_9-\kappa c_7
\]

in all D4 wall formulas.

This makes the rank selector basis-invariant up to bounded row changes.

---

# 9. Current theorem

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{RowNormalForm}_{2Y}.
}
\]

Assume:

\[
\ell_7(e_7)=1,\quad \ell_7(e_Y)=0,
\]

\[
\omega_Y(e_Y)=1,\quad |\omega_Y(e_7)|\le Q^A,
\]

\[
\|\ell_7\|+\|\omega_Y\|\le Q^A.
\]

Then the row-normalized pair

\[
\ell_7,\quad
\ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\omega_Y(e_7)\ell_7
\]

is polynomially bounded and satisfies

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),
\]

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1).
\]

If both rows kill \(A_{2Y}\), then

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

The D4 matrix in this row basis is

\[
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7&0\\
\beta_9-\omega_Y(e_7)c_7&\alpha_9
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

---

# 10. Endpoint

Row triangularization is now part of the exact rank-two formalism.

It provides three concrete benefits:

1. It relaxes the \(Y12\)-row audit: \(\omega_Y(e_7)\) may be nonzero.
2. It prevents false graph-slope detection before branch classification.
3. It gives the correct coefficient

\[
\beta_9^{\tri}
\]

for both rank-two coefficient-good routing and rank-one fallback.

The rank-two wall remains the plain wall

\[
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})
\]

up to the triangularized coefficient matrix.

## Agent 3 — \(\mathrm{NuisancePreservesRows}_{2Y}\)

No nomenclature changes.

I will keep the correction explicit: this is a **local algebra audit**. If \(B_Y=0\), D4 supplies only the honest plain wall

\[
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}),
\]

up to the triangularized coefficient matrix. No derivative wall components are being used.

The assigned task is to prove or falsify nuisance row-deadness and live-pair preservation:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0,
\qquad
\omega_Y(P_W\mathcal N_{\sing})=0,
\]

while preserving the live rows

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)\neq 0,
\qquad
a_Y\notin W_{\dead}^{\fixed},
\]

and avoiding any two-grade collapse

\[
e_Y-\lambda e_7\in D_{2Y}+\mathcal N_{\sing}.
\]

This is one of the remaining local blockers for \(B_Y=0\). 

---

# 1. What nuisance is allowed to contain

The nuisance space

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
\]

should include only dead or representative degrees of freedom. It may contain:

\[
\mathcal N_{\chart},
\quad
\mathcal N_{\gauge},
\quad
\mathcal N_{\norm},
\quad
\mathcal N_{\tail},
\quad
\mathcal N_{\wrongorigin},
\quad
\mathcal N_{\transported}.
\]

More explicitly:

- chart/center/scale representative directions;
- gauge and normalization redundancies;
- tail/truncation residual representatives;
- wrong-origin order-nine artifacts;
- transported formed-source artifacts;
- true singular nuisance directions that are deliberately quotiented.

It must **not** contain live corrected-package coordinates:

\[
e_7,
\qquad
e_Y,
\qquad
e_Y-\lambda e_7
\]

unless the proof intentionally abandons rank two or changes the carrier.

So the nuisance audit is a denominator-balance problem:

\[
\boxed{
\text{large enough to kill dead representative drift, small enough to preserve }(e_7,e_Y).
}
\]

---

# 2. \(s_7\)-row nuisance deadness

The \(s_7\)-row is

\[
\ell_7(u)=
\frac{\Delta_7^\nabla(u)}{\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)}.
\]

Thus nuisance is \(s_7\)-dead iff

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0.
}
\]

Equivalently,

\[
\boxed{
Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal N_{\sing}
\subseteq
\mathbb C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
}
\]

This says all nuisance order-seven package motion lies on the canonical quotient line and has no transverse septic package scalar.

The live \(C_7\)-row must still survive modulo nuisance:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}
\left(
Q_7^{\pkg}C_7,\,
\mathbb C A_5^{\mathfrak f}+Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal N_{\sing}
\right)
\ge Q^{-A}\|C_7\|.
}
\]

In finite matrix form:

\[
\operatorname{rank}[A_5^{\mathfrak f},Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal N_{\sing}]=1,
\]

but

\[
\operatorname{rank}[A_5^{\mathfrak f},Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal N_{\sing},Q_7^{\pkg}C_7]=2
\]

with polynomial separation.

---

# 3. \(s_9^Y\)-row nuisance deadness

The primitive \(Y12\)-row is read by the whitening covector

\[
\omega_Y
\]

before triangularization.

Nuisance is \(Y12\)-dead iff

\[
\boxed{
P_W\mathcal N_{\sing}\subseteq W_{\dead}^{\fixed}.
}
\]

Equivalently,

\[
\boxed{
\omega_Y(P_W\mathcal N_{\sing})=0.
}
\]

The live \(Y12\) carrier must survive modulo nuisance:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}
\left(
a_Y,\,
W_{\dead}^{\fixed}+P_W\mathcal N_{\sing}
\right)
\ge Q^{-A}\|a_Y\|.
}
\]

This prevents nuisance from killing the live same-point whitening slot.

If nuisance has an element with live \(a_Y\)-component, then it either kills the \(Y12\) row outright or collapses the row into actual/nuisance coherence. That must be treated as quotient overcollapse unless it is a deliberate carrier change.

---

# 4. Two-grade homogeneity

Even if each row separately looks safe, nuisance may still relate the two live grades. The dangerous relation is

\[
\boxed{
e_Y-\lambda e_7\in D_{2Y}+\mathcal N_{\sing}.
}
\]

This would identify the primitive order-nine \(Y12\) carrier with the order-seven \(C_7\) carrier in the quotient. Then the live pair is not rank two.

So we need either a direct-sum decomposition

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal N_{\sing}
=
\mathcal N_7\oplus \mathcal N_9
}
\]

with respect to

\[
J_{7,9}=J_7\oplus J_9,
\]

or at least a block-triangular condition whose off-diagonal part is typed-dead and cannot contain

\[
e_Y-\lambda e_7.
\]

A finite test is:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}
\left(
e_Y,\,
D_{2Y}+\mathcal N_{\sing}+\mathbb C e_7
\right)
\ge Q^{-A}\|e_Y\|.
}
\]

This is the live-pair preservation condition in the two-grade quotient.

---

# 5. Interaction with row normal form

Recall row normal form:

\[
\ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\kappa\ell_7,
\qquad
\kappa=\omega_Y(e_7),
\]

and

\[
\beta_9^{\tri}=\beta_9-\kappa c_7.
\]

If nuisance is row-dead for the preliminary rows,

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0,
\qquad
\omega_Y(P_W\mathcal N_{\sing})=0,
\]

then it is also dead for the triangularized second row:

\[
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal N_{\sing})
=
\omega_Y(\mathcal N_{\sing})-\kappa\ell_7(\mathcal N_{\sing})
=
0.
\]

So nuisance deadness is stable under triangularization.

But if nuisance causes

\[
\ell_7(e_Y)\neq0,
\]

that is not removable by triangularizing the second row. That is a structural two-grade quotient failure.

---

# 6. Allowed nuisance versus overcollapse

## Allowed nuisance

Nuisance is acceptable if it lies in the dead typed denominator:

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
\subseteq
\mathcal D_{2Y},
\]

and satisfies the row-deadness tests.

Examples:

\[
\text{chart/gauge representative changes},
\]

\[
\text{normalization line shifts},
\]

\[
\text{transported EF-linear artifacts},
\]

\[
\text{wrong-origin order-nine terms},
\]

\[
\text{residual representatives}.
\]

## Overcollapse

Nuisance overcollapses if it contains or nearly contains:

\[
e_7,
\]

\[
e_Y,
\]

\[
e_Y-\lambda e_7,
\]

or a nontrivial transverse \(Q_7^{\pkg}\)-class.

Then rank two fails by quotient design, not by arithmetic absorption.

---

# 7. Failure routing

## Failure A — nuisance has transverse \(s_7\)

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal N_{\sing})\neq0.
\]

Then the first D4 row is quotiented as nuisance. Either this is a genuine gauge redundancy, in which case \(C_7\) should not be used as live row, or it is overcollapse.

Route:

\[
\text{refine nuisance or abandon the \(C_7\)-row.}
\]

## Failure B — nuisance has primitive \(Y12\)

\[
\omega_Y(P_W\mathcal N_{\sing})\neq0.
\]

Then the \(Y12\) row is killed or made nuisance-coherent.

Route:

\[
\text{refine }W_{\dead}^{\fixed}/\mathcal N_{\sing}\text{ or abandon this }Y12\text{ carrier.}
\]

## Failure C — nuisance identifies \(e_Y\) with \(e_7\)

\[
e_Y-\lambda e_7\in D_{2Y}+\mathcal N_{\sing}.
\]

Then rank two collapses to at most rank one before any actual-source audit.

Route:

\[
\text{repair two-grade quotient or use rank-one fallback if meaningful.}
\]

## Failure D — row norms blow up

Rows exist algebraically but have super-polynomial norm. Then live-pair conditioning fails.

Route:

\[
\text{refine basis/quotient or restrict to better-conditioned charts.}
\]

---

# 8. The theorem

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{NuisancePreservesRows}_{2Y}.
}
\]

Assume:

1. \(s_7\)-row nuisance deadness:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal N_{\sing}
\subseteq
\mathbb C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

2. \(C_7\) survives modulo nuisance:

\[
\operatorname{dist}
\left(
Q_7^{\pkg}C_7,\,
\mathbb C A_5^{\mathfrak f}+Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal N_{\sing}
\right)
\ge Q^{-A}\|C_7\|.
\]

3. \(Y12\)-row nuisance deadness:

\[
P_W\mathcal N_{\sing}\subseteq W_{\dead}^{\fixed}.
\]

4. \(a_Y\) survives modulo nuisance:

\[
\operatorname{dist}
\left(
a_Y,\,
W_{\dead}^{\fixed}+P_W\mathcal N_{\sing}
\right)
\ge Q^{-A}\|a_Y\|.
\]

5. Two-grade independence:

\[
\operatorname{dist}
\left(
e_Y,\,
D_{2Y}+\mathcal N_{\sing}+\mathbb C e_7
\right)
\ge Q^{-A}\|e_Y\|.
\]

Then nuisance contributes no \(B_Y\)-column and preserves the rank-two live pair.

Together with row exactness for actual data and no-atom-fitting for \(E_2/R_{\lab}\), this supports

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

---

# 9. Current endpoint

The nuisance problem is now a finite row-preservation audit:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0,
\quad
\omega_Y(P_W\mathcal N_{\sing})=0,
}
\]

plus live-pair separation:

C_7\notin \mathcal D+\mathcal N,
\qquad
a_Y\notin \mathcal D+\mathcal N,
\qquad

}

## Agent 3 continuation — nuisance preservation as a Schur complement, not a semantic quotient claim

I continue with fixed notation:

\[
\rho_Y=\text{the }Y12\text{-fiber graph slope},\qquad
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9},\qquad
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.
\]

The current target is

\[
\mathrm{NuisancePreservesRows}_{2Y}.
\]

The key point is that nuisance preservation should not be treated as a verbal assertion that “nuisance is harmless.” It must be a finite Schur/angle statement in the same two-grade quotient used to build the live rows. The correction remains: if this closes and \(B_Y=0\), D4 gives only the honest plain wall \(H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})\), not derivative wall components. 

---

# 1. Nuisance preservation is a two-part condition

There are two logically different requirements.

## Part A — row-deadness

Nuisance must not create actual/local directions in the live rows:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0,
\]

\[
\omega_Y(P_W\mathcal N_{\sing})=0.
\]

This says nuisance contributes no \(B_Y\)-column.

## Part B — live survival

Nuisance must not kill or identify the live D4 carriers:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}C_7\notin \mathbb C A_5^{\mathfrak f}+Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal N_{\sing},
\]

\[
a_Y\notin W_{\dead}^{\fixed}+P_W\mathcal N_{\sing},
\]

\[
e_Y\notin D_{2Y}+\mathcal N_{\sing}+\mathbb C e_7.
\]

This says the live pair remains two-dimensional after quotienting.

Both parts are required. Row-deadness alone is insufficient: a nuisance direction could be row-dead but still collapse the quotient by identifying \(e_Y\) with \(e_7\).

---

# 2. Finite Schur form for the nuisance audit

Define the nuisance matrix

\[
N_{2Y}
=
[N_7\mid N_9],
\]

where \(N_7\) collects order-seven nuisance columns and \(N_9\) order-nine nuisance columns, both expressed in the two-grade carrier space.

Let

\[
D_{2Y}^{0}
\]

be the typed dead denominator before adding nuisance. Then the full denominator is

\[
D_{2Y}
=
D_{2Y}^{0}+N_{2Y}.
\]

The live pair is

\[
L_{2Y}=[e_7,e_Y].
\]

The exact finite Schur condition is

\[
\boxed{
\lambda_{\min}
\left(
L_{2Y}^{*}(I-P_{D_{2Y}})L_{2Y}
\right)
\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]

But in row language, it is enough to construct bounded rows \(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y\) that kill \(D_{2Y}\) and normalize \(e_7,e_Y\).

Thus nuisance preservation can be certified either by the Gram condition above or by the bounded row conditions:

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0,\qquad
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0,
\]

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),
\qquad
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1),
\]

\[
\|\ell_7\|+\|\ell_9^Y\|\le Q^A.
\]

The row certificate is preferable because it simultaneously proves row-deadness and live survival.

---

# 3. The \(s_7\)-nuisance test

The order-seven nuisance condition is

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal N_{\sing}
\subseteq
\mathbb C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

Equivalently,

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0.
\]

This should hold for nuisance generated by:

\[
\text{chart/center/scale representatives},
\]

\[
\text{normalization/gauge line shifts},
\]

\[
\text{tail/truncation representatives},
\]

\[
\text{EF-linear or transported artifacts}.
\]

All such terms should be dead because they either lie on the \(A_5^{\mathfrak f}\)-line or have wrong corrected-package type.

The dangerous nuisance is a transverse corrected septic scalar:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}n\notin \mathbb C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

If such \(n\in\mathcal N_{\sing}\) exists, then nuisance kills the \(C_7\)-row. That is not an arithmetic defeat; it means the quotient has overcollapsed the D4 carrier.

The finite test is:

\[
\operatorname{rank}
[A_5^{\mathfrak f},Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal N_{\sing}]
=
1,
\]

while

\[
\operatorname{rank}
[A_5^{\mathfrak f},Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal N_{\sing},Q_7^{\pkg}C_7]
=
2
\]

with polynomial angle.

---

# 4. The \(s_9^Y\)-nuisance test

The \(Y12\)-row nuisance condition is

\[
P_W\mathcal N_{\sing}
\subseteq
W_{\dead}^{\fixed}.
\]

Equivalently,

\[
\omega_Y(P_W\mathcal N_{\sing})=0.
\]

This should hold for nuisance generated by:

\[
\text{transported whitening artifacts},
\]

\[
\text{chart/gauge whitening drift},
\]

\[
\text{normalization/background terms},
\]

\[
\text{wrong-origin same-point terms}.
\]

The dangerous nuisance is one with a live same-point \(a_Y\)-component:

\[
P_{D_W^\perp}P_W n\ne0.
\]

If this exists, nuisance touches the primitive \(Y12\)-row. Again, that is quotient overcollapse unless this \(Y12\) carrier was intentionally declared nuisance.

The finite test is:

\[
P_W\mathcal N_{\sing}\subseteq W_{\dead}^{\fixed},
\]

and

\[
\operatorname{dist}
\left(
a_Y,
W_{\dead}^{\fixed}+P_W\mathcal N_{\sing}
\right)
\ge Q^{-A}\|a_Y\|.
\]

---

# 5. Two-grade mixing is the subtle nuisance failure

Even if the row tests pass separately, a nuisance relation may identify the two grades:

\[
e_Y-\lambda e_7\in D_{2Y}^{0}+\mathcal N_{\sing}.
\]

This is the hidden killer. It can make the live pair one-dimensional while each row audit looks superficially acceptable.

So we need a direct two-grade angle condition:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}
\left(
e_Y,
D_{2Y}^{0}+\mathcal N_{\sing}+\mathbb C e_7
\right)
\ge Q^{-A}\|e_Y\|.
}
\]

This is stronger than saying \(a_Y\) survives in the whitening quotient, because it prevents the order-nine carrier from being represented by an order-seven carrier plus nuisance.

In typed terms, it requires nuisance to be grade-homogeneous or block triangular with no live off-diagonal component:

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
=
\mathcal N_7\oplus\mathcal N_9
\]

or

\[
\operatorname{offdiag}(\mathcal N_{\sing})
\subset D_{2Y}^{0}
\]

with no projection onto \(e_Y-\lambda e_7\).

---

# 6. Row-normal-form interaction

Using row normal form,

\[
\ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\kappa\ell_7,
\qquad
\kappa=\omega_Y(e_7),
\]

nuisance deadness is stable:

if

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0,
\qquad
\omega_Y(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0,
\]

then

\[
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0.
\]

So nuisance can be audited using the preliminary rows \(\ell_7,\omega_Y\), and then triangularization preserves the result.

But row-normal form does **not** fix a nuisance relation

\[
e_Y-\lambda e_7\in D+\mathcal N.
\]

That relation destroys live-pair independence before row normalization.

Therefore the audit order should be:

1. prove \(e_7,e_Y\) survive independently modulo nuisance;
2. build bounded rows;
3. triangularize;
4. verify nuisance row-deadness in the triangular basis.

---

# 7. NuisancePreservesRows\(_{2Y}\) theorem

A clean theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\textbf{NuisancePreservesRows}_{2Y}.
}
\]

Assume:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal N_{\sing}
\subseteq
\mathbb C A_5^{\mathfrak f},
\]

\[
\operatorname{dist}
\left(
Q_7^{\pkg}C_7,
\mathbb C A_5^{\mathfrak f}+Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal N_{\sing}
\right)
\ge Q^{-A}\|C_7\|,
\]

\[
P_W\mathcal N_{\sing}
\subseteq
W_{\dead}^{\fixed},
\]

\[
\operatorname{dist}
\left(
a_Y,
W_{\dead}^{\fixed}+P_W\mathcal N_{\sing}
\right)
\ge Q^{-A}\|a_Y\|,
\]

and

\[
\operatorname{dist}
\left(
e_Y,
D_{2Y}^{0}+\mathcal N_{\sing}+\mathbb C e_7
\right)
\ge Q^{-A}\|e_Y\|.
\]

Then nuisance is row-dead and live-pair preserving. In particular, after row normal form,

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0,
\]

and the normalized live pair remains

\[
(e_7,e_Y)\mapsto ((1,0),(0,1)).
\]

Thus nuisance contributes nothing to \(B_Y\) and does not collapse rank two.

---

# 8. Failure implications

## Failure A: transverse septic nuisance

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal N_{\sing})\ne0.
\]

The \(C_7\)-row is nuisance. Rank two cannot use \(C_7\) unless nuisance is refined.

## Failure B: primitive \(Y12\) nuisance

\[
\omega_Y(P_W\mathcal N_{\sing})\ne0.
\]

The \(Y12\)-row is nuisance. Current \(Y12\) carrier is overquotiented.

## Failure C: two-grade identification

\[
e_Y-\lambda e_7\in D+\mathcal N.
\]

Rank two collapses to rank one or worse before actual-source analysis.

## Failure D: poor angle

Rows exist but with super-polynomial norm. The rank-two lower is too ill-conditioned.

---

# 9. Current endpoint

The nuisance audit is now a finite preservation theorem:

\[
\boxed{
\text{nuisance must be row-dead and must not collapse }(e_7,e_Y).
}
\]

This is one of the few remaining local blockers.

If \(\mathrm{NuisancePreservesRows}_{2Y}\) passes, and the \(s_7\)-row, \(Y12\)-row, and no-atom-fitting modules pass, then the bounded dual-row certificate gives

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0.
}
\]

Then the rank-two branch proceeds with the honest plain wall

\[
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]
